1 # Memory Architecture Design 2 3 > **Status:** Implemented (Core), Planned (Graph-Based Hybrid) 4 > **Updated:** 2026-01-27 5 6 Local embeddings + lightweight sidecar (GPT-5.3 Codex Spark) are implemented and running in production. This document describes both the current implementation and the planned graph-based hybrid architecture. 7 8 ## Overview 9 10 See also: [Memory Regression Budget](./MEMORY_BUDGET.md) for the current measurable guardrails and review expectations. 11 12 A multi-layered memory system for cross-session learning that mimics how human memory works - relevant memories "pop up" when triggered by context rather than requiring explicit recall. 13 14 **Key Design Decisions:** 15 1. **Fully async and non-blocking** - The main agent never waits for memory; results from turn N are available at turn N+1 16 2. **Graph-based organization** - Memories form a connected graph with tags, clusters, and semantic links 17 3. **Cascade retrieval** - Embedding hits trigger BFS traversal to find related memories 18 4. **Hybrid grouping** - Combines explicit tags, automatic clusters, and semantic links 19 20 --- 21 22 ## Architecture Overview 23 24 ```mermaid 25 graph TB 26 subgraph "Main Agent" 27 MA[TUI App] 28 MP[build_memory_prompt] 29 TP[take_pending_memory] 30 end 31 32 subgraph "Memory Agent" 33 CH[Context Handler] 34 EMB[Embedder
all-MiniLM-L6-v2] 35 SR[Similarity Search] 36 CR[Cascade Retrieval] 37 HC[Sidecar
GPT-5.3 Codex Spark] 38 end 39 40 subgraph "Memory Graph" 41 MG[(petgraph
DiGraph)] 42 MS[Memory Nodes] 43 TN[Tag Nodes] 44 CN[Cluster Nodes] 45 end 46 47 MA -->|mpsc channel| CH 48 CH --> EMB 49 EMB --> SR 50 SR -->|initial hits| CR 51 CR -->|BFS traversal| MG 52 MG --> MS 53 MG --> TN 54 MG --> CN 55 CR -->|candidates| HC 56 HC -->|verified| TP 57 TP -->|next turn| MA 58 ``` 59 60 ---

# Memory Architecture Design > **Status:** Implemented (Core), Planned (Graph-Based Hybrid) > **Updated:** 2026-01-27 Local embeddings + lightweight sidecar (GPT-5.3 Codex Spark) are implemented and running in production. This document describes both the current implementation and the planned graph-based hybrid architecture. ## Overview See also: [Memory Regression Budget](./MEMORY_BUDGET.md) for the current measurable guardrails and review expectations. A multi-layered memory system for cross-session learning that mimics how human memory works - relevant memories "pop up" when triggered by context rather than requiring explicit recall. **Key Design Decisions:** 1. **Fully async and non-blocking** - The main agent never waits for memory; results from turn N are available at turn N+1 2. **Graph-based organization** - Memories form a connected graph with tags, clusters, and semantic links 3. **Cascade retrieval** - Embedding hits trigger BFS traversal to find related memories 4. **Hybrid grouping** - Combines explicit tags, automatic clusters, and semantic links --- ## Architecture Overview ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Main Agent" MA[TUI App] MP[build_memory_prompt] TP[take_pending_memory] end subgraph "Memory Agent" CH[Context Handler] EMB[Embedder
all-MiniLM-L6-v2] SR[Similarity Search] CR[Cascade Retrieval] HC[Sidecar
GPT-5.3 Codex Spark] end subgraph "Memory Graph" MG[(petgraph
DiGraph)] MS[Memory Nodes] TN[Tag Nodes] CN[Cluster Nodes] end MA -->|mpsc channel| CH CH --> EMB EMB --> SR SR -->|initial hits| CR CR -->|BFS traversal| MG MG --> MS MG --> TN MG --> CN CR -->|candidates| HC HC -->|verified| TP TP -->|next turn| MA ``` ---

=== contributors === 1 Jeremy Huang === last commit + count (shallow, limited) === 2026-06-22 08:04:38 -0700 92a17ea tui: recall queued prompts on Ctrl/Alt/Cmd+Up (not just Ctrl+Up) total commits in shallow clone: 1 === version === version = "0.31.2" === SAFETY head === # Safety System > **Status:** Design > **Updated:** 2026-02-08 A human-in-the-loop safety layer for unmonitored agent operations. Designed as an independent subsystem that any jcode feature can integrate with. Currently the only consumer is ambient mode, but the system is intentionally decoupled so it can be reused for future features. ## Overview When an agent operates without direct user supervision (e.g. ambient mode), it needs a way to: 1. **Know what it's allowed to do** without asking 2. **Request permission** for actions that require human approval 3. **Notify the user** that a request is pending 4. **Wait or move on** while the user reviews 5. **Report what it did** after each session The safety system provides all of this. There are only two tiers: auto-allowed and requires-permission. There is no "always denied" — if the user explicitly approves something, the agent can do it. The core principle is that **anything that communicates with another human or leaves a trace outside the local sandbox requires permission.** --- ## Architecture ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Agent (e.g. Ambient Mode)" A[Agent wants to take action] AC{Action classification} AUTO[Auto-allowed

content.text:
Letto a fondo. Ecco il quadro per decidere — **senza ancora toccare il tuo workspace** (la copia è in scratchpad temp).

## Cos'è, in sostanza
Coding agent harness Rust, MIT, **single-author** (Jeremy Huang), molto attivo (ultimo commit 22-giu-2026, 3gg fa). ~**277k LOC** produzione, 68 crate. Categoria = Codex CLI / Claude Code, non un modello.

## Architettura (il cuore)
- **Single-server, multi-client**: un daemon `jcode serve` su Unix socket (`/run/user/$UID/jcode.sock`) tiene tutte le sessioni + provider pool + MCP pool condivisi; i client TUI si attaccano/staccano, reconnect con backoff, **hot-reload** (`/reload` → exec nuovo binario stesso PID). Da qui i numeri RAM/boot bassi.
- **Memory semantica**: embedding locale `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` + grafo `petgraph`, retrieval a cascata (BFS), sidecar verifica rilevanza. Async non-bloccante (turn N → disponibile N+1). **Questo è il valore aggiunto vero** vs Codex/Claude Code. Costo: +140 MB RAM quando embedding ON (27→167 MB).
- **Swarm**: N agenti stesso repo, server notifica file-shift, messaging A↔B, auto-coordinamento.
- **Safety**: 2 tier (auto-allow / chiede-permesso); regola = "qualunque cosa esca dal sandbox locale o parli con umano → permesso".

## Maturità — onesto
- ✅ `clippy -D warnings` pulito, fmt pulito, no `dead_code`, ~5k LOC test.
- ⚠️ **1258 `unwrap`/`expect` in produzione** (136 in un solo file `tool/communicate.rs`) → panic potenziali. 50 file >1200 LOC, 304 funzioni >100 LOC → debito strutturale.
- ⚠️ Discrepanza versione (Cargo `0.31.2` vs badge `v0.9.1888-dev`) — versioning non rigoroso. Niente release stabile vera. **Bus factor = 1**.

## Angolo integrazione + free
- **BYO-model totale**: crate dedicate `provider-{anthropic,openai,gemini,openrouter,copilot,bedrock,antigravity}` + ~40 profili OpenAI-compatible. Free reale: **Ollama/LM Studio locale** (0 costo) o **OpenRouter `:free`**.
- Audit provider (`provider-model-catalog-audit.md`) è onesto: per Ollama/Z.AI/Perplexity `/v1/models` non verificato → catalogo fallback statico, possibili attriti.
- Integrabile via **MCP** (`.claude/mcp.json` presente) e **hooks** (`docs/HOOKS.md`) — si aggancia a sistemi esistenti senza fork.

## Verdetto
Vale come **esperimento personale / sostituto leggero di Codex CLI con memory** — combacia col tuo toolchain (OpenRouter `ccr` + Gemini + Ollama già in `toolchain-llm-clis`). **Non** maturo per "integrarlo nei sistemi" prod: single-author, 1200+ unwrap, zero release stabile, churn alto. Zero lock-in però (MIT + BYO).

---
Prossimo passo, scegli:
- **(1)** Builda in scratchpad + cablo su Ollama/OpenRouter free → lo provi davvero prima di clonare nel workspace
- **(2)** Approfondisco un sottosistema specifico (memory / swarm / hooks-MCP per integrazione)
- **(3)** Salvo memoria `reference-jcode` con questa analisi così non la rifacciamo
- **(4)** Stop qui, ti basta il quadro

content.text: Build avviato (output bufferizzato fino a fine, normale). Aspetto la notifica di completamento — niente polling. Ti aggiorno appena finisce con esito + cablaggio gemma4.

message.content:
Ovviamente l'obiettivo è implementare questo J Code interamente, sostituendo Hermes con il modello in locale che alimenta Gemma 4 o un modello locale futuro.

L'intento è valutarne effettivamente gli impatti per capire se sono positivi, visto che in teoria dovrebbe essere molto più leggero e quindi più veloce nella risposta, nelle valutazioni, ecc. Ovviamente, se serve, cortesemente fai un backup o ricorda in memoria gli switch che stai facendo, perché qualora ci trovassimo male dobbiamo ritornare su Hermes.

Lo switch vero (Hermes→jcode) avverrà solo dopo eval positiva, e prima di quello faccio backup config Hermes.

Per documentare bene rollback, localizzo prima com'è configurato Hermes oggi:

content.content:
1	---
2	name: hermes-agent-docs
3	description: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — docs, repo, local source checkout, key CLI/slash commands for our local-Ollama setup.
4	metadata:
5	  type: reference
6	---
7	
8	# Hermes Agent — docs & pointers
9	
10	Agent powering pocket-llm's local coding TUI (see [[pocket-llm]]). MIT, by Nous Research.
11	
12	## Links
13	- Repo: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
14	- Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/ (quickstart, CLI, configuration, tools, skills, memory, MCP, cron, context-files, architecture, env-vars)
15	- Install one-liner: `curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash`
16	
17	## Local install (authoritative for OUR installed version — read this, not the web, offline + 0 chat tokens)
18	- Home: `~/.hermes` (config.yaml, .env, SOUL.md, sessions DB, hooks, skills).
19	- **Source git checkout**: `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` — the SAME tree `hermes update` pulls. Any local
20	  source patch (e.g. customizing `/resume`) is OVERWRITTEN on update → avoid patching; prefer config/flags.
21	- TUI vs classic: `hermes --tui` (modern TUI, interactive picker overlays) vs `hermes --cli` (classic REPL).
22	  Also set via `display.interface: tui` in config.yaml. `_parser.py` defines both flags.
23	
24	## Key commands / slash
25	- CLI: `hermes` (chat), `hermes model`, `hermes tools`, `hermes skills`, `hermes sessions`, `hermes config set`,
26	  `hermes doctor` (diagnose), `hermes update`, `hermes prompt-size` (STATIC max budget, not live prefill).
27	- Slash: `/model`, `/personality [name]`, `/new`·`/reset`, `/retry`·`/undo`, `/compress`, `/usage`,
28	  `/insights [--days N]`, `/skills`, `/resume <number|id|title>` (bare `/resume` lists → type just the number).
29	- Interrupt: `Ctrl+C` or send a new message.
30	- Context files (cwd, prompt_builder.py `build_context_files_prompt`): loads ONE, first-found by priority
31	  `HERMES.md`/`.hermes.md` (walk to git root) > `AGENTS.md` > `CLAUDE.md` > `.cursorrules`, PLUS `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` always.
32	  All are PREFILL. Workspace root `HERMES.md` (tracked via `git add -f`; ignore-all repo) is our Hermes-only context:
33	  tuned persona (Italian, concise/aseptic/functional, speed>verbosity, one line before multi-step actions) + active
34	  projects. It SHADOWS the caveman `AGENTS.md` and the 8KB `CLAUDE.md` for Hermes (neither reaches Hermes).
35	  `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` now carries a MINIMAL global Italian-default persona (Reply in Italian by default, concise/
36	  aseptic, switch only on user cue) — because HERMES.md only loads when the cwd (up to its git root) has it, so
37	  opening any OTHER project would drop the Italian directive; SOUL.md is always loaded, so it guarantees the default
38	  everywhere (set 2026-06-23, supersedes the earlier "leave SOUL.md empty" note). Kept minimal to avoid duplicating
39	  the fuller HERMES.md persona. SOUL.md reloads fresh each message (no restart). It now also adds grounding
40	  (read/use tools before acting, don't invent file contents/APIs), work hygiene (only the lines asked for, verify
41	  before closing, 127.0.0.1), safety (destructive→confirm), and Italian-orthography (accents, never ASCII). The
42	  richer distributed persona lives in `pocket-llm/hermes/SOUL.md` (a template, aligned to this lean version via
43	  PR #12 2026-06-23). **SOUL.md ≠ CLAUDE.md**: SOUL.md is the always-on persona (prefill EVERY message → keep lean);
44	  the real CLAUDE.md analog is the per-cwd `HERMES.md` (verbose project rules, loaded only in that repo).
45	  Loader path (verified 2026-06-23): `agent/prompt_builder.py::load_soul_md` reads `~/.hermes/SOUL.md`, `.strip()`,
46	  if non-empty returns it → `agent/system_prompt.py:155-157` appends to `stable_parts` = system-prompt identity slot #1.
47	  Verify it's actually loaded by running Hermes' OWN function (system python lacks `yaml` — use the Hermes venv):
48	  `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/python -c "import sys;sys.path.insert(0,'.');from agent.prompt_builder import load_soul_md;print(len(load_soul_md(16384) or ''))"`
49	  (run from `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`; non-zero len = loaded). Confirmed: 849 chars, Italian+grounding+safety all present.
50	  Don't edit CLAUDE.md (shared brain) or AGENTS.md (generic, other agents).
51	
52	## What we deliberately disable for prefill/speed (all re-activatable — see pocket-llm/vscode-extension/HERMES.md)
53	Memory learning-loop, agent-curated memory, FTS5 session search, skills (71 removed), delegation/subagents,
54	gateway (Telegram/Discord/Slack), cron, vision/tts/browser/image_gen tools. README's headline features =
55	exactly these; our build trades the self-improving loop for a snappy local agent.
56	
57	## DON'T re-attempt: TUI status-bar customization (settled 2026-06-18)
58	The `--tui` status bar (`model/ctx/timer · 📁cwd (branch)`) is HARDCODED in the compiled React TUI
59	(`ui-tui`, cwd/branch from `fmtCwdBranch` in `ui-tui/src/domain/paths.ts`, rendered by
60	`components/appChrome.tsx`). There is NO supported config/hook/plugin API to change its layout — open
61	feature requests confirm it: NousResearch/hermes-agent#13490, #17540, #17543. The only way is to patch
62	core source + `npm run build` the bundle, which `hermes update` reverts (a fragile workaround). We TRIED
63	it (worked: `📁 …/AI Projects ⎇ master`) then REVERTED — user wants native-or-nothing, no forced
64	workaround. Native bar already shows folder+branch. `gemma4:12b` ctx shows model max (262.1k) not our
65	runtime `num_ctx` — cosmetic. If a future Hermes adds `/statusbar config` (the requested feature), use that.
66	
67	## Switching the local model — provider is `custom`, NOT `ollama` (settled 2026-06-23)
68	A local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint is a **`custom`** provider to Hermes. Labeling it `provider: ollama`
69	in config.yaml logs `Unknown provider 'ollama'` and silently FALLS BACK to auto-detection / the previously
70	cached model. Working block:
71	```yaml
72	model:
73	  default: <ollama-tag>      # e.g. gemma4:12b
74	  provider: custom
75	  api_key: ollama            # any non-empty string; Ollama ignores it
76	  base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
77	```
78	**Editing `model.default` alone is NOT enough**: Hermes validates the tag against its model-picker disk cache
79	(`~/.hermes/provider_models_cache.json`, `context_length_cache.yaml`); a tag absent from cache → fallback to the
80	old model (verified: a pulled-but-uncached tag ran the previous model despite the config edit). Fix via
81	`hermes model` (interactive, needs a TTY): pick the saved `custom`/`Local (127.0.0.1:11434)` provider → it
82	re-fetches live `/v1/models` → select the tag. `hermes model --refresh` wipes+rebuilds the cache. After an
83	`ollama pull` of a NEW tag, re-run the picker so Hermes sees it. The picker also rewrites config.yaml
84	(alphabetizes keys, adds a `custom_providers:` entry). `hermes model` cannot be run non-interactively (errors
85	"requires an interactive terminal") → guide the user to run it.
86	
87	## TWO SEPARATE LANES via Hermes PROFILES (2026-06-24, supersedes "single shared default = agy")
88	Two independent agents that must NOT step on each other: **local gemma4** (Chip / pocket-llm extension) vs
89	**cloud Gemini+router** (FreeCode / ccr-free-router robot button). Earlier both ran plain `hermes` → ONE shared
90	`~/.hermes/config.yaml`, ONE default → whichever launched LAST won the default (a launch race even made one fall
91	back to gemma4 while the other kept agy). They also couldn't have different prefill weights (gemma4 needs a LEAN
92	prefill). **FIX = Hermes' native `profile` feature** (`hermes profile create/list/use/alias`; each profile =
93	`~/.hermes/profiles/<name>/` with its OWN config.yaml + SOUL.md + skills; the shared CODE/plugin patch stays in
94	`~/.hermes/hermes-agent`, so the thinking/temperature patch covers both lanes automatically):
95	- **`default` profile** (`~/.hermes`) → **gemma4:12b** local (Ollama :11434). LEAN prefill (no bundled skills).
96	  Launched by the **Chip** pocket-llm extension's plain `hermes --tui` — pocket-llm untouched.
97	- **`openrouter` profile** (`~/.hermes/profiles/openrouter`, wrapper cmd `~/.local/bin/openrouter`) → **agy /
98	  Gemini 3.5 Flash** (agy shim :3457) + router. Richer prefill (71 bundled skills). Launched by **freecode**,
99	  which now runs `openrouter --tui` (ccr-free-router PR #1, falls back to `hermes`); the robot button runs freecode.
100	- Created via `hermes profile create openrouter`; its config seeded by copying the working agy `config.yaml`, then
101	  the `default` config flipped back to gemma4. Name is model-agnostic ("openrouter" — today gemini+OpenRouter free,
102	  could be other models later). Per-profile `SOUL.md`/`HERMES.md` = where you tune each lane's persona/prefill.
103	  Both profiles' `SOUL.md` carry the SAME lean Italian brevity backbone (concise/direct, expand only when asked,
104	  code complete, grounding, safety) — 2026-06-24 — with distinct framing (local coder vs cloud daily-driver):
105	  "one agent body, two profile souls". Each soul's TEMPLATE is tracked in its lane's repo for reproducibility:
106	  gemma4 → `pocket-llm/hermes/SOUL.md`; Gemini → `ccr-free-router/hermes/SOUL.md` (PR #2). Live files:
107	  `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` + `~/.hermes/profiles/openrouter/SOUL.md` (untracked). NB the real gemma4 speed lever is
108	  OUTPUT brevity (decode-bound ~7.6 tok/s), NOT prefill size (prompt-eval ~7300 tok/s = near-free), so "richer
109	  prefill for the cloud lane" was dropped; both stay lean.
110	Verified: `hermes status` → gemma4:12b · `openrouter status` → Gemini 3.5 Flash (Medium); `hermes profile list`
111	shows both. Old single-default note (the agy shim still wraps Antigravity CLI `agy`; `GET /v1/models` added in
112	commit `4fe24c0`; backups `config.yaml.pre-agy.bak`/`.pre-separate.bak`) now applies only to the `openrouter` lane.
113	- **Launcher**: `freecode` lives in **`ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`** (tracked, sourced by `~/.zshrc`; NOT
114	  inlined) = guard-if-no-`hermes` + `_ccr_shim_up` + `hermes --tui`. The VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
115	  `vscode-launcher`, v0.1.0) now launches `freecode` too (shellArgs `-lic freecode`, prewarms the agy shim, status
116	  bar reads `agy-state.json`, menu "Switch HERMES model"→`hermes model`) — terminal and button behave identically.
117	  User decision: ALL the launcher orchestration goes in ccr-free-router; do NOT touch the pocket-llm repo/extension
118	  (that's a separate gemma4-local project). The intended replacement for ever typing `claude`/`ccr code` daily.
119	- **Caveat (same wall as ccr code)**: the shim runs `agy -p` = TEXT-ONLY, no native `tool_use`. So Hermes'
120	  agentic tool-loop over agy is degraded (chat/simple turns fine; gemma4 local DOES emit tool_calls via Ollama
121	  → switch to it via `hermes model` for tool-heavy work). agy ~7-15s/call. To restore failover/tools, switch
122	  the primary back to gemma4 or point at CCR :3456.
123	
124	## gemma4 thinking control — SOLVED via plugin patch (2026-06-23, supersedes "can't be disabled")
125	gemma4:12b is hybrid-thinking (Ollama capability `thinking`). Earlier it seemed unsuppressible; the real chain:
126	- Ollama's OpenAI **`/v1`** IGNORES the native `think` flag and `chat_template_kwargs`, but (Ollama ≥0.30.x)
127	  **HONORS `reasoning_effort`**: `none` → 0 reasoning (off), `low`/`medium`/`high` → thinking on. Verified by curl.
128	- Hermes' custom-provider plugin (`plugins/model-providers/custom/__init__.py`) emitted only `think:false`
129	  when effort=none → ineffective on `/v1`. And `_supports_reasoning_extra_body` (run_agent.py) returns False for
130	  any non-OpenRouter base_url, so Hermes never sent `reasoning_effort` to a local Ollama custom provider.
131	- System-prompt directives DON'T work (tested: gemma4 reasons ABOUT a "never think" rule instead of obeying).
132	**FIX (applied)**: patched `plugins/model-providers/custom/__init__.py` `build_api_kwargs_extras` to map the
133	reasoning effort onto Ollama's param: effort `none`/disabled → `extra_body["think"]=False` + `["reasoning_effort"]="none"`;
134	any other effort → `extra_body["reasoning_effort"]=<effort>`. Now the toggle works end-to-end:
135	- `/reasoning none` → thinking OFF (fast, direct) · `/reasoning low|medium|high` → thinking ON at that level.
136	- `/reasoning hide` still just hides the block from display (compute unaffected).
137	The SAME plugin patch ALSO sets balanced sampling (PR #14, 2026-06-24): returns `{"temperature":0.6,"top_p":0.9}`
138	as the TOP-LEVEL kwargs (2nd tuple element), not in `extra_body.options`. Why top-level: Ollama `/v1` honors
139	top-level OpenAI `temperature`/`top_p` (temp 0 → identical outputs) but IGNORES `options.temperature` (verified by
140	determinism test). gemma4's Ollama default temp is 1.0 (too high → rambly, less deterministic code); 0.6 is the
141	all-purpose middle ground (tighter code, still fine for chat). Returned as profile defaults so `request_overrides`
142	still win. Carried by the canonical copy + reapply script, so it survives `hermes update` together with the toggle.
143	**⚠ This is a SOURCE patch in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (NOT a tracked repo) → `hermes update` OVERWRITES it.
144	Re-apply after every update** — but now AUTOMATED: the VS Code launcher runs the reapply script on every
145	launch BEFORE opening the TUI (pocket-llm PR #15, 2026-06-24, ext v0.1.1), so a fresh launch self-heals the
146	patch and the new process loads it — no manual re-apply, no "restart to activate" (the restart caveat only ever
147	applied because Python plugins import once at process start; launching IS the activation). Manual path stays a
148	ONE-COMMAND durable installer (pocket-llm, merged PR #11 2026-06-23):
149	`bash ~/Documents/AI\ Projects/pocket-llm/scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh` (idempotent: checks Ollama≥0.30,
150	skips if already patched, backs up live plugin, copies canonical `pocket-llm/hermes/custom_provider_patched.py`,
151	ast-validates). Default config now `agent.reasoning_effort: none` (thinking off). Exact change inside
152	`build_api_kwargs_extras`:
153	```python
154	if _effort == "none" or _enabled is False:
155	    extra_body["think"] = False
156	    extra_body["reasoning_effort"] = "none"   # added — Ollama /v1 honors this, not `think`
157	elif _effort:
158	    extra_body["reasoning_effort"] = _effort   # added — low/medium/high
159	```
160	Requires Ollama ≥0.30.x. Long-term: file/track upstream so the custom/Ollama provider sends `reasoning_effort`.
161	
162	**Triggers:** hermes, hermes agent, nous research, hermes docs, hermes tui, hermes config, hermes update, hermes commands, hermes model, provider custom, ollama provider, unknown provider, model switch, model cache refresh, thinking, reasoning effort, /reasoning, disable thinking, gemma4 think
163	**Why:** the installed source is the source of truth (offline, version-exact); web README is cloud/Portal-oriented and mostly N/A to our local-Ollama trimmed build.
164	**How to apply:** for any Hermes question, read `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (source) + `~/.hermes/config.yaml`; don't patch source (update overwrites) — change config/flags. Open docs URL only for conceptual guides.
165

file.content:
---
name: hermes-agent-docs
description: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — docs, repo, local source checkout, key CLI/slash commands for our local-Ollama setup.
metadata:
  type: reference
---

# Hermes Agent — docs & pointers

Agent powering pocket-llm's local coding TUI (see [[pocket-llm]]). MIT, by Nous Research.

## Links
- Repo: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
- Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/ (quickstart, CLI, configuration, tools, skills, memory, MCP, cron, context-files, architecture, env-vars)
- Install one-liner: `curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash`

## Local install (authoritative for OUR installed version — read this, not the web, offline + 0 chat tokens)
- Home: `~/.hermes` (config.yaml, .env, SOUL.md, sessions DB, hooks, skills).
- **Source git checkout**: `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` — the SAME tree `hermes update` pulls. Any local
  source patch (e.g. customizing `/resume`) is OVERWRITTEN on update → avoid patching; prefer config/flags.
- TUI vs classic: `hermes --tui` (modern TUI, interactive picker overlays) vs `hermes --cli` (classic REPL).
  Also set via `display.interface: tui` in config.yaml. `_parser.py` defines both flags.

## Key commands / slash
- CLI: `hermes` (chat), `hermes model`, `hermes tools`, `hermes skills`, `hermes sessions`, `hermes config set`,
  `hermes doctor` (diagnose), `hermes update`, `hermes prompt-size` (STATIC max budget, not live prefill).
- Slash: `/model`, `/personality [name]`, `/new`·`/reset`, `/retry`·`/undo`, `/compress`, `/usage`,
  `/insights [--days N]`, `/skills`, `/resume <number|id|title>` (bare `/resume` lists → type just the number).
- Interrupt: `Ctrl+C` or send a new message.
- Context files (cwd, prompt_builder.py `build_context_files_prompt`): loads ONE, first-found by priority
  `HERMES.md`/`.hermes.md` (walk to git root) > `AGENTS.md` > `CLAUDE.md` > `.cursorrules`, PLUS `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` always.
  All are PREFILL. Workspace root `HERMES.md` (tracked via `git add -f`; ignore-all repo) is our Hermes-only context:
  tuned persona (Italian, concise/aseptic/functional, speed>verbosity, one line before multi-step actions) + active
  projects. It SHADOWS the caveman `AGENTS.md` and the 8KB `CLAUDE.md` for Hermes (neither reaches Hermes).
  `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` now carries a MINIMAL global Italian-default persona (Reply in Italian by default, concise/
  aseptic, switch only on user cue) — because HERMES.md only loads when the cwd (up to its git root) has it, so
  opening any OTHER project would drop the Italian directive; SOUL.md is always loaded, so it guarantees the default
  everywhere (set 2026-06-23, supersedes the earlier "leave SOUL.md empty" note). Kept minimal to avoid duplicating
  the fuller HERMES.md persona. SOUL.md reloads fresh each message (no restart). It now also adds grounding
  (read/use tools before acting, don't invent file contents/APIs), work hygiene (only the lines asked for, verify
  before closing, 127.0.0.1), safety (destructive→confirm), and Italian-orthography (accents, never ASCII). The
  richer distributed persona lives in `pocket-llm/hermes/SOUL.md` (a template, aligned to this lean version via
  PR #12 2026-06-23). **SOUL.md ≠ CLAUDE.md**: SOUL.md is the always-on persona (prefill EVERY message → keep lean);
  the real CLAUDE.md analog is the per-cwd `HERMES.md` (verbose project rules, loaded only in that repo).
  Loader path (verified 2026-06-23): `agent/prompt_builder.py::load_soul_md` reads `~/.hermes/SOUL.md`, `.strip()`,
  if non-empty returns it → `agent/system_prompt.py:155-157` appends to `stable_parts` = system-prompt identity slot #1.
  Verify it's actually loaded by running Hermes' OWN function (system python lacks `yaml` — use the Hermes venv):
  `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/python -c "import sys;sys.path.insert(0,'.');from agent.prompt_builder import load_soul_md;print(len(load_soul_md(16384) or ''))"`
  (run from `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`; non-zero len = loaded). Confirmed: 849 chars, Italian+grounding+safety all present.
  Don't edit CLAUDE.md (shared brain) or AGENTS.md (generic, other agents).

## What we deliberately disable for prefill/speed (all re-activatable — see pocket-llm/vscode-extension/HERMES.md)
Memory learning-loop, agent-curated memory, FTS5 session search, skills (71 removed), delegation/subagents,
gateway (Telegram/Discord/Slack), cron, vision/tts/browser/image_gen tools. README's headline features =
exactly these; our build trades the self-improving loop for a snappy local agent.

## DON'T re-attempt: TUI status-bar customization (settled 2026-06-18)
The `--tui` status bar (`model/ctx/timer · 📁cwd (branch)`) is HARDCODED in the compiled React TUI
(`ui-tui`, cwd/branch from `fmtCwdBranch` in `ui-tui/src/domain/paths.ts`, rendered by
`components/appChrome.tsx`). There is NO supported config/hook/plugin API to change its layout — open
feature requests confirm it: NousResearch/hermes-agent#13490, #17540, #17543. The only way is to patch
core source + `npm run build` the bundle, which `hermes update` reverts (a fragile workaround). We TRIED
it (worked: `📁 …/AI Projects ⎇ master`) then REVERTED — user wants native-or-nothing, no forced
workaround. Native bar already shows folder+branch. `gemma4:12b` ctx shows model max (262.1k) not our
runtime `num_ctx` — cosmetic. If a future Hermes adds `/statusbar config` (the requested feature), use that.

## Switching the local model — provider is `custom`, NOT `ollama` (settled 2026-06-23)
A local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint is a **`custom`** provider to Hermes. Labeling it `provider: ollama`
in config.yaml logs `Unknown provider 'ollama'` and silently FALLS BACK to auto-detection / the previously
cached model. Working block:
```yaml
model:
  default: <ollama-tag>      # e.g. gemma4:12b
  provider: custom
  api_key: ollama            # any non-empty string; Ollama ignores it
  base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
```
**Editing `model.default` alone is NOT enough**: Hermes validates the tag against its model-picker disk cache
(`~/.hermes/provider_models_cache.json`, `context_length_cache.yaml`); a tag absent from cache → fallback to the
old model (verified: a pulled-but-uncached tag ran the previous model despite the config edit). Fix via
`hermes model` (interactive, needs a TTY): pick the saved `custom`/`Local (127.0.0.1:11434)` provider → it
re-fetches live `/v1/models` → select the tag. `hermes model --refresh` wipes+rebuilds the cache. After an
`ollama pull` of a NEW tag, re-run the picker so Hermes sees it. The picker also rewrites config.yaml
(alphabetizes keys, adds a `custom_providers:` entry). `hermes model` cannot be run non-interactively (errors
"requires an interactive terminal") → guide the user to run it.

## TWO SEPARATE LANES via Hermes PROFILES (2026-06-24, supersedes "single shared default = agy")
Two independent agents that must NOT step on each other: **local gemma4** (Chip / pocket-llm extension) vs
**cloud Gemini+router** (FreeCode / ccr-free-router robot button). Earlier both ran plain `hermes` → ONE shared
`~/.hermes/config.yaml`, ONE default → whichever launched LAST won the default (a launch race even made one fall
back to gemma4 while the other kept agy). They also couldn't have different prefill weights (gemma4 needs a LEAN
prefill). **FIX = Hermes' native `profile` feature** (`hermes profile create/list/use/alias`; each profile =
`~/.hermes/profiles/<name>/` with its OWN config.yaml + SOUL.md + skills; the shared CODE/plugin patch stays in
`~/.hermes/hermes-agent`, so the thinking/temperature patch covers both lanes automatically):
- **`default` profile** (`~/.hermes`) → **gemma4:12b** local (Ollama :11434). LEAN prefill (no bundled skills).
  Launched by the **Chip** pocket-llm extension's plain `hermes --tui` — pocket-llm untouched.
- **`openrouter` profile** (`~/.hermes/profiles/openrouter`, wrapper cmd `~/.local/bin/openrouter`) → **agy /
  Gemini 3.5 Flash** (agy shim :3457) + router. Richer prefill (71 bundled skills). Launched by **freecode**,
  which now runs `openrouter --tui` (ccr-free-router PR #1, falls back to `hermes`); the robot button runs freecode.
- Created via `hermes profile create openrouter`; its config seeded by copying the working agy `config.yaml`, then
  the `default` config flipped back to gemma4. Name is model-agnostic ("openrouter" — today gemini+OpenRouter free,
  could be other models later). Per-profile `SOUL.md`/`HERMES.md` = where you tune each lane's persona/prefill.
  Both profiles' `SOUL.md` carry the SAME lean Italian brevity backbone (concise/direct, expand only when asked,
  code complete, grounding, safety) — 2026-06-24 — with distinct framing (local coder vs cloud daily-driver):
  "one agent body, two profile souls". Each soul's TEMPLATE is tracked in its lane's repo for reproducibility:
  gemma4 → `pocket-llm/hermes/SOUL.md`; Gemini → `ccr-free-router/hermes/SOUL.md` (PR #2). Live files:
  `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` + `~/.hermes/profiles/openrouter/SOUL.md` (untracked). NB the real gemma4 speed lever is
  OUTPUT brevity (decode-bound ~7.6 tok/s), NOT prefill size (prompt-eval ~7300 tok/s = near-free), so "richer
  prefill for the cloud lane" was dropped; both stay lean.
Verified: `hermes status` → gemma4:12b · `openrouter status` → Gemini 3.5 Flash (Medium); `hermes profile list`
shows both. Old single-default note (the agy shim still wraps Antigravity CLI `agy`; `GET /v1/models` added in
commit `4fe24c0`; backups `config.yaml.pre-agy.bak`/`.pre-separate.bak`) now applies only to the `openrouter` lane.
- **Launcher**: `freecode` lives in **`ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`** (tracked, sourced by `~/.zshrc`; NOT
  inlined) = guard-if-no-`hermes` + `_ccr_shim_up` + `hermes --tui`. The VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  `vscode-launcher`, v0.1.0) now launches `freecode` too (shellArgs `-lic freecode`, prewarms the agy shim, status
  bar reads `agy-state.json`, menu "Switch HERMES model"→`hermes model`) — terminal and button behave identically.
  User decision: ALL the launcher orchestration goes in ccr-free-router; do NOT touch the pocket-llm repo/extension
  (that's a separate gemma4-local project). The intended replacement for ever typing `claude`/`ccr code` daily.
- **Caveat (same wall as ccr code)**: the shim runs `agy -p` = TEXT-ONLY, no native `tool_use`. So Hermes'
  agentic tool-loop over agy is degraded (chat/simple turns fine; gemma4 local DOES emit tool_calls via Ollama
  → switch to it via `hermes model` for tool-heavy work). agy ~7-15s/call. To restore failover/tools, switch
  the primary back to gemma4 or point at CCR :3456.

## gemma4 thinking control — SOLVED via plugin patch (2026-06-23, supersedes "can't be disabled")
gemma4:12b is hybrid-thinking (Ollama capability `thinking`). Earlier it seemed unsuppressible; the real chain:
- Ollama's OpenAI **`/v1`** IGNORES the native `think` flag and `chat_template_kwargs`, but (Ollama ≥0.30.x)
  **HONORS `reasoning_effort`**: `none` → 0 reasoning (off), `low`/`medium`/`high` → thinking on. Verified by curl.
- Hermes' custom-provider plugin (`plugins/model-providers/custom/__init__.py`) emitted only `think:false`
  when effort=none → ineffective on `/v1`. And `_supports_reasoning_extra_body` (run_agent.py) returns False for
  any non-OpenRouter base_url, so Hermes never sent `reasoning_effort` to a local Ollama custom provider.
- System-prompt directives DON'T work (tested: gemma4 reasons ABOUT a "never think" rule instead of obeying).
**FIX (applied)**: patched `plugins/model-providers/custom/__init__.py` `build_api_kwargs_extras` to map the
reasoning effort onto Ollama's param: effort `none`/disabled → `extra_body["think"]=False` + `["reasoning_effort"]="none"`;
any other effort → `extra_body["reasoning_effort"]=<effort>`. Now the toggle works end-to-end:
- `/reasoning none` → thinking OFF (fast, direct) · `/reasoning low|medium|high` → thinking ON at that level.
- `/reasoning hide` still just hides the block from display (compute unaffected).
The SAME plugin patch ALSO sets balanced sampling (PR #14, 2026-06-24): returns `{"temperature":0.6,"top_p":0.9}`
as the TOP-LEVEL kwargs (2nd tuple element), not in `extra_body.options`. Why top-level: Ollama `/v1` honors
top-level OpenAI `temperature`/`top_p` (temp 0 → identical outputs) but IGNORES `options.temperature` (verified by
determinism test). gemma4's Ollama default temp is 1.0 (too high → rambly, less deterministic code); 0.6 is the
all-purpose middle ground (tighter code, still fine for chat). Returned as profile defaults so `request_overrides`
still win. Carried by the canonical copy + reapply script, so it survives `hermes update` together with the toggle.
**⚠ This is a SOURCE patch in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (NOT a tracked repo) → `hermes update` OVERWRITES it.
Re-apply after every update** — but now AUTOMATED: the VS Code launcher runs the reapply script on every
launch BEFORE opening the TUI (pocket-llm PR #15, 2026-06-24, ext v0.1.1), so a fresh launch self-heals the
patch and the new process loads it — no manual re-apply, no "restart to activate" (the restart caveat only ever
applied because Python plugins import once at process start; launching IS the activation). Manual path stays a
ONE-COMMAND durable installer (pocket-llm, merged PR #11 2026-06-23):
`bash ~/Documents/AI\ Projects/pocket-llm/scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh` (idempotent: checks Ollama≥0.30,
skips if already patched, backs up live plugin, copies canonical `pocket-llm/hermes/custom_provider_patched.py`,
ast-validates). Default config now `agent.reasoning_effort: none` (thinking off). Exact change inside
`build_api_kwargs_extras`:
```python
if _effort == "none" or _enabled is False:
    extra_body["think"] = False
    extra_body["reasoning_effort"] = "none"   # added — Ollama /v1 honors this, not `think`
elif _effort:
    extra_body["reasoning_effort"] = _effort   # added — low/medium/high
```
Requires Ollama ≥0.30.x. Long-term: file/track upstream so the custom/Ollama provider sends `reasoning_effort`.

**Triggers:** hermes, hermes agent, nous research, hermes docs, hermes tui, hermes config, hermes update, hermes commands, hermes model, provider custom, ollama provider, unknown provider, model switch, model cache refresh, thinking, reasoning effort, /reasoning, disable thinking, gemma4 think
**Why:** the installed source is the source of truth (offline, version-exact); web README is cloud/Portal-oriented and mostly N/A to our local-Ollama trimmed build.
**How to apply:** for any Hermes question, read `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (source) + `~/.hermes/config.yaml`; don't patch source (update overwrites) — change config/flags. Open docs URL only for conceptual guides.

content.content: Launching skill: save-memory

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/raimondospatola/.claude/skills/save-memory

# Save Memory

Single dispatcher that persists durable knowledge so future sessions draw from it instead of re-researching. The memory lives in `.claude/` and is indexed by `.claude/MEMORY.md`. Scope is this workspace only (AI Projects: nexus-mind, personal config) — never persist anything tied to external work contexts.

## Step 0 — Gate check (exit fast)

Persist ONLY durable, reusable knowledge. Skip (do NOT save) when:
- It is structural info the codebase already owns (files, functions, deps) — regenerate from code instead.
- It is ephemeral task detail (use a plan / task list, not memory).
- It duplicates an existing memory (update that file instead — see Step 4).

Save when one matches:
- **Durable rule/preference** for future sessions ("d'ora in poi", "from now on", "sempre/mai").
- **Correction** not to repeat ("non farlo più così", "stop doing X").
- **Confirmed-approach default** ("è andata bene, ricordatelo").
- **New project state** outliving the task (in-flight ticket, infra setup, deployed component).
- **External reference** (URL, dashboard, ID).
- **User-profile fact**.

Unsure → err toward saving; this skill skips on duplicates / ambiguous scope.

## Step 1 — Pick type (file prefix)

| Type | Prefix | For |
|---|---|---|
| feedback | `feedback_<topic>.md` | how we work / why we decided — conventions, corrections, confirmed defaults |
| project | `project_<name>.md` | architecture & active state of a project |
| reference | `reference_<topic>.md` | external resource pointers (URL, API, dashboard, env) |
| user | `user_<name>.md` | user-profile facts, preferences |

## Step 2 — Frontmatter + body

Match the convention already used by existing `.claude/*_*.md` files:

```markdown
---
name: <kebab-case-slug>
description: <one-line summary used for retrieval relevance>
metadata:
  type: feedback | project | reference | user
---

# <Title>

<the fact>

[[related-memory-slug]]   ← cross-link liberally

**Triggers:** comma-separated retrieval keywords
**Why:** why this matters
**How to apply:** when/how to use it next session
```

- Convert relative dates to absolute (e.g. "today" → the actual date).
- Secrets NEVER as literal values — reference env vars only.

## Step 3 — Index it in MEMORY.md

Add a one-line pointer under the right section of `.claude/MEMORY.md`:
`- [<file>.md](<file>.md) — <hook>.`
Never put the full memory content in MEMORY.md — index only.

## Step 4 — Dedup & cross-link

- Before creating, check for an existing file covering the topic → **update it** rather than duplicating.
- Add `[[slug]]` cross-links to related memories; isolated nodes weaken retrieval.
- Delete a memory that turns out wrong.

## Step 5 — Sync

Per the Config Self-Sync rule in CLAUDE.md: after writing, `git add -A && git commit -m "docs(memory): <what>" && git push origin master`.

## Announcement

When invoked by the dispatcher (not typed `/save-memory`), announce on one line before acting — IT: *"Sto avviando la skill `save-memory`."*


ARGUMENTS: Progetto: valutazione jcode come sostituto leggero di Hermes su gemma4 locale. Documenta esperimento in corso + baseline rollback Hermes.
Sto avviando la skill `save-memory`. Creo `project_jcode_eval.md` in `$WS/.claude/`, indicizzo, cross-link a Hermes/pocket-llm.

---
name: jcode-eval
description: Evaluating jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) as a lighter replacement for Hermes driving local gemma4. In-flight experiment + Hermes rollback baseline.
metadata:
  type: project
---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model
coding harness, driving `gemma4:12b` on Ollama (or a future local model). Hypothesis: jcode is
much lighter/faster (Rust, ~27 MB RAM embedding-off vs Hermes' Python/Node stack), so response
and eval latency should improve. Evaluate REAL impact before committing.

## What jcode is
- Repo: https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode — MIT, single-author (Jeremy Huang), very active.
- Rust coding-agent harness (Codex/Claude-Code category), NOT an LLM. BYO-model: ~40 OpenAI-compatible
  providers + native Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter. Free path = local Ollama / LM Studio / OpenRouter `:free`.
- Ollama profile: api_base `http://localhost:11434/v1`, auth "local endpoint" (no key), default_model none
  → must pass `--model gemma4:12b`. Login: `jcode login --provider ollama` then
  `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '<prompt>'`.
- Differentiators vs Hermes: single-server multi-client daemon (Unix socket, hot-reload), semantic
  memory (local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings + petgraph), swarm (multi-agent same repo), MCP + hooks.
- Maturity caveats: ~277k LOC, **1258 prod `unwrap`/`expect`** (panic risk), 50 files >1200 LOC,
  version mismatch (Cargo 0.31.2 vs badge v0.9.1888-dev), no real stable release, bus factor 1.
  → fine as personal experiment, risky for "prod" reliance.

## Experiment state (2026-06-25)
- Clone: scratchpad temp only (`.../scratchpad/jcode`), NOT in `$WS`. 503 MB.
- Installed **Rust 1.96.0 via Homebrew** (`brew install rust`, pulled `pkgconf` dep) — was absent.
  Removable via `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` if abandoning.
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` (background, long). Next: login ollama + smoke-test gemma4.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.

## Rollback baseline — Hermes (DO NOT switch until eval passes + user OK)
Current Hermes = the thing jcode would replace. See [[hermes-agent-docs]] for full config. Key points:
- Home `~/.hermes`: `config.yaml`, `SOUL.md`, profiles, source checkout `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`.
- Two profiles: `default` → gemma4:12b local (Chip / pocket-llm ext); `openrouter` → agy/Gemini (FreeCode).
- Launchers: `freecode` in `ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`; VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  vscode-launcher) runs `freecode`. The real cutover = repoint these launchers/button from Hermes to jcode.
- Source patch (thinking/temp) in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` via pocket-llm reapply script.
- **Before any cutover**: back up `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (+ profiles) and snapshot launcher files so we
  can revert to Hermes if jcode underperforms.

[[hermes-agent-docs]] [[pocket-llm]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode eval, replace hermes, lighter harness, gemma4 jcode, ollama jcode, rust coding agent, 1jehuang, cutover hermes, rollback hermes
**Why:** user wants to A/B jcode vs Hermes for the local coding lane; needs a clean revert path if jcode loses.
**How to apply:** continue the experiment from "Experiment state"; never cutover launchers without backing up `~/.hermes` first + explicit user approval. If abandoning jcode, `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + delete scratchpad clone.

5	- [user_raimondo_spatola.md](user_raimondo_spatola.md) — profile, language/style prefs, "brain first", session-memory preference.

7	## 📂 Active projects
8	- [reference_active_projects.md](reference_active_projects.md) — local projects, macOS env, Node/Python, default port, memory paths, config-sync remote + auto-sync.
9	- [reference_gemini_cli.md](reference_gemini_cli.md) — Gemini REST (`gemini-3.5-flash`): bash invocation, parallel fan-out, when to dispatch as cross-model worker. Key format now `AQ.` (not `AIza` — obsolete).

12	- [reference_ccr_openrouter.md](reference_ccr_openrouter.md) — CCR free-only: **Gemini 3.5 Flash PRIMARY** (own key) + cross-provider failover to OpenRouter free via custom router (`ccr-router.js`) + `gemini-failover.js` (429=quota long / 503=overload short cooldown). OpenRouter side = self-healing `fallback-models.js` with **power matrix/classifica** (best-1M + best-lower, score→params ranking). Privacy data-policy gotcha. Tracked in `.claude/ccr/`.
13	- [reference_ccr_free_router_repo.md](reference_ccr_free_router_repo.md) — private GitHub repo `RaggioS/ccr-free-router` = ACTIVE dev repo for the CCR free-LLM setup. Chain: **Antigravity CLI `agy` (Gemini 3.5 Flash, personal OAuth) → OpenRouter** via `agy-shim.js` local provider, + UI-selection override hook (commit `18c71be`). gemini-cli STILL dead (IneligibleTier→Antigravity) but its replacement `agy` works → wrapped; old Gemini REST path dropped. Verified live e2e 2026-06-24.
14	- [reference_claude_md.md](reference_claude_md.md) — CLAUDE.md project setup and task guidance.

16	## 💻 Engineering & Frontend
17	- [feedback_frontend_preferences.md](feedback_frontend_preferences.md) — dark HSL, glassmorphism, Lucide, Vanilla CSS, React patterns, lint/build gate.
18	- [feedback_git_workflow.md](feedback_git_workflow.md) — conventional commits EN, branch naming, rebase from main, files not to commit (`session_state.json`); **worktree-per-session/task** (1 session=1 worktree=1 branch=1 PR; same-dir = shared index/branch corruption; `/wt-task` skill A/B; standing rule: `.worktrees/` in every project repo's `.gitignore` (safe PR flow). Validated e2e 2026-06-19 → MEMORY_ARCHIVE.
19	- [feedback_bilingual_localization_swarm_memory.md](feedback_bilingual_localization_swarm_memory.md) — EN/IT i18n, `titleEn`/`titleIt` props, 7-section recap, auto-archive at Step 10.
20	> **Engineering standards**: document every component (never empty desc); never hardcode IDs (metadata/labels/dynamic lookups); no queries/writes in loops; handlers delegate to handler classes; check existing component before creating; every PR a product-oriented summary (value/behaviour/impact, not just mechanics; 2-4 sentences) + assignee `RaggioS` — never empty body/bare title; confirm scope (1-2 sentences) before writing code.

23	- [feedback_pipeline_review.md](feedback_pipeline_review.md) — 2-phase Pipeline Review: A ideation, B post-code; self-review scope; severity discipline.
24	- [feedback_subagent_orchestration.md](feedback_subagent_orchestration.md) — tier delegation, inline vs sub-agent thresholds, brief (announce type+model+task before dispatch).
25	- [feedback_deployment_lifecycle.md](feedback_deployment_lifecycle.md) — env dev→staging→prod, no-auto-deploy, pre-deploy checklist, no-polling.
26	- [feedback_plan_mode_discipline.md](feedback_plan_mode_discipline.md) — never auto-switch plan; declare assumptions before acting; 3-layer task tracking.
27	- [feedback_bash_cwd_hooks.md](feedback_bash_cwd_hooks.md) — never `cd` in compound Bash (moves persistent CWD → breaks relative-path hooks → deadlock); use subshell; keep hook paths absolute via `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`.

29

# Swarm Memory Index — read first every session, before codebase
Retrieval: CLAUDE.md → MEMORY.md → `<type>_<topic>.md` on demand.

## 👤 Profile
- [user_raimondo_spatola.md](user_raimondo_spatola.md) — profile, language/style prefs, "brain first", session-memory preference.

## 📂 Active projects
- [reference_active_projects.md](reference_active_projects.md) — local projects, macOS env, Node/Python, default port, memory paths, config-sync remote + auto-sync.
- [reference_gemini_cli.md](reference_gemini_cli.md) — Gemini REST (`gemini-3.5-flash`): bash invocation, parallel fan-out, when to dispatch as cross-model worker. Key format now `AQ.` (not `AIza` — obsolete).

- [reference_caveman.md](reference_caveman.md) — Caveman token-compression: modes/commands, macOS install/uninstall, config paths. **Installed, full always on.**
- [reference_ccr_openrouter.md](reference_ccr_openrouter.md) — CCR free-only: **Gemini 3.5 Flash PRIMARY** (own key) + cross-provider failover to OpenRouter free via custom router (`ccr-router.js`) + `gemini-failover.js` (429=quota long / 503=overload short cooldown). OpenRouter side = self-healing `fallback-models.js` with **power matrix/classifica** (best-1M + best-lower, score→params ranking). Privacy data-policy gotcha. Tracked in `.claude/ccr/`.
- [reference_ccr_free_router_repo.md](reference_ccr_free_router_repo.md) — private GitHub repo `RaggioS/ccr-free-router` = ACTIVE dev repo for the CCR free-LLM setup. Chain: **Antigravity CLI `agy` (Gemini 3.5 Flash, personal OAuth) → OpenRouter** via `agy-shim.js` local provider, + UI-selection override hook (commit `18c71be`). gemini-cli STILL dead (IneligibleTier→Antigravity) but its replacement `agy` works → wrapped; old Gemini REST path dropped. Verified live e2e 2026-06-24.
- [reference_claude_md.md](reference_claude_md.md) — CLAUDE.md project setup and task guidance.

## 💻 Engineering & Frontend
- [feedback_frontend_preferences.md](feedback_frontend_preferences.md) — dark HSL, glassmorphism, Lucide, Vanilla CSS, React patterns, lint/build gate.
- [feedback_git_workflow.md](feedback_git_workflow.md) — conventional commits EN, branch naming, rebase from main, files not to commit (`session_state.json`); **worktree-per-session/task** (1 session=1 worktree=1 branch=1 PR; same-dir = shared index/branch corruption; `/wt-task` skill A/B; standing rule: `.worktrees/` in every project repo's `.gitignore` (safe PR flow). Validated e2e 2026-06-19 → MEMORY_ARCHIVE.
- [feedback_bilingual_localization_swarm_memory.md](feedback_bilingual_localization_swarm_memory.md) — EN/IT i18n, `titleEn`/`titleIt` props, 7-section recap, auto-archive at Step 10.
> **Engineering standards**: document every component (never empty desc); never hardcode IDs (metadata/labels/dynamic lookups); no queries/writes in loops; handlers delegate to handler classes; check existing component before creating; every PR a product-oriented summary (value/behaviour/impact, not just mechanics; 2-4 sentences) + assignee `RaggioS` — never empty body/bare title; confirm scope (1-2 sentences) before writing code.

## 🤖 AI protocol
- [feedback_pipeline_review.md](feedback_pipeline_review.md) — 2-phase Pipeline Review: A ideation, B post-code; self-review scope; severity discipline.
- [feedback_subagent_orchestration.md](feedback_subagent_orchestration.md) — tier delegation, inline vs sub-agent thresholds, brief (announce type+model+task before dispatch).
- [feedback_deployment_lifecycle.md](feedback_deployment_lifecycle.md) — env dev→staging→prod, no-auto-deploy, pre-deploy checklist, no-polling.
- [feedback_plan_mode_discipline.md](feedback_plan_mode_discipline.md) — never auto-switch plan; declare assumptions before acting; 3-layer task tracking.
- [feedback_bash_cwd_hooks.md](feedback_bash_cwd_hooks.md) — never `cd` in compound Bash (moves persistent CWD → breaks relative-path hooks → deadlock); use subshell; keep hook paths absolute via `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`.
- [feedback_file_deletion_safety.md](feedback_file_deletion_safety.md) — never delete Claude/ccr-adjacent files (hooks, `.claude/**`, tooling symlinks like caveman, ccr config); only explicit unambiguous junk, ask first otherwise.

- [project_subscription_sharing.md](project_subscription_sharing.md) — learning MVP distributed inference mesh (FastAPI coordinator + WS workers, toggle, mock backend); private repo, own remote; roadmap.
- [reference_distributed_inference.md](reference_distributed_inference.md) — RAM-pooling tools (exo / DwarfStar-ds4 / Petals) + quantization reality (DeepSeek-V4 284B can't fit 8GB at any quant; bandwidth = bottleneck, tensor-parallel non-viable on consumer net).
- [project_pocket_llm.md](project_pocket_llm.md) — local Hermes coding agent on Ollama **gemma4:12b** (VS Code launcher); qwen3-vl:8b evaluated & rejected 2026-06-23 (gemma4 wins benchmarks + thinking variant too slow on 16GB); private repo, own remote; symlink shared-config pattern.
- [reference_hermes_agent.md](reference_hermes_agent.md) — Hermes Agent docs/repo + local source `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (don't patch, update overwrites); `--tui` vs `--cli`, key slash/CLI commands, what we disabled for prefill; **local model = `custom` provider (not `ollama`), switch via `hermes model` (cache-validated); gemma4 thinking toggle SOLVED via custom-provider plugin patch (Ollama≥0.30 /v1 honors `reasoning_effort`; `/reasoning none|low|medium|high` works; re-apply patch after `hermes update`)**. **PRIMARY model now = agy (Antigravity Gemini) via the agy-shim, NOT gemma4 (2026-06-24); launcher `freecode` (~/.zshrc) = HERMES sole daily driver, no Claude Code; gemma4 = switchable offline fallback. Text-only caveat (no tool_use over agy).**
- [reference_odysseus_eval.md](reference_odysseus_eval.md) — Odysseus (PewDiePie self-hosted web AI workspace, NOT an LLM) evaluated as full Hermes replacement → **NOT adopted** (2026-06-25): both lanes aggancieable (Ollama gemma4 + OpenRouter/agy custom endpoint), but paradigm mismatch (browser workspace vs editor-embedded TUI) → coexistence > replacement. Pointer kept in case we revisit.


## 🧩 Skills / Commands / Agents (synced in repo)
- Skills (`.claude/skills/`): save-memory, session-recap, self-review, ticket-close.
- Commands (`.claude/commands/`): /five, /markitdown, /mermaid, /todo, /ux.
- Agents (`.claude/agents/`): coder-reviewer, frontend-engineer, project-manager, tech-lead-architect, ux-designer.

---
name: jcode-eval
description: Evaluating jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) as a lighter replacement for Hermes driving local gemma4. In-flight experiment + Hermes rollback baseline.
metadata:
  type: project
---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model
coding harness, driving `gemma4:12b` on Ollama (or a future local model). Hypothesis: jcode is
much lighter/faster (Rust, ~27 MB RAM embedding-off vs Hermes' Python/Node stack), so response
and eval latency should improve. Evaluate REAL impact before committing.

## What jcode is
- Repo: https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode — MIT, single-author (Jeremy Huang), very active.
- Rust coding-agent harness (Codex/Claude-Code category), NOT an LLM. BYO-model: ~40 OpenAI-compatible
  providers + native Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter. Free path = local Ollama / LM Studio / OpenRouter `:free`.
- Ollama profile: api_base `http://localhost:11434/v1`, auth "local endpoint" (no key), default_model none
  → must pass `--model gemma4:12b`. Login: `jcode login --provider ollama` then
  `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '<prompt>'`.
- Differentiators vs Hermes: single-server multi-client daemon (Unix socket, hot-reload), semantic
  memory (local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings + petgraph), swarm (multi-agent same repo), MCP + hooks.
- Maturity caveats: ~277k LOC, **1258 prod `unwrap`/`expect`** (panic risk), 50 files >1200 LOC,
  version mismatch (Cargo 0.31.2 vs badge v0.9.1888-dev), no real stable release, bus factor 1.
  → fine as personal experiment, risky for "prod" reliance.

## Experiment state (2026-06-25)
- Clone: scratchpad temp only (`.../scratchpad/jcode`), NOT in `$WS`. 503 MB.
- Installed **Rust 1.96.0 via Homebrew** (`brew install rust`, pulled `pkgconf` dep) — was absent.
  Removable via `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` if abandoning.
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` (background, long). Next: login ollama + smoke-test gemma4.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.

## Rollback baseline — Hermes (DO NOT switch until eval passes + user OK)
Current Hermes = the thing jcode would replace. See [[hermes-agent-docs]] for full config. Key points:
- Home `~/.hermes`: `config.yaml`, `SOUL.md`, profiles, source checkout `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`.
- Two profiles: `default` → gemma4:12b local (Chip / pocket-llm ext); `openrouter` → agy/Gemini (FreeCode).
- Launchers: `freecode` in `ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`; VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  vscode-launcher) runs `freecode`. The real cutover = repoint these launchers/button from Hermes to jcode.
- Source patch (thinking/temp) in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` via pocket-llm reapply script.
- **Before any cutover**: back up `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (+ profiles) and snapshot launcher files so we
  can revert to Hermes if jcode underperforms.

[[hermes-agent-docs]] [[pocket-llm]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode eval, replace hermes, lighter harness, gemma4 jcode, ollama jcode, rust coding agent, 1jehuang, cutover hermes, rollback hermes
**Why:** user wants to A/B jcode vs Hermes for the local coding lane; needs a clean revert path if jcode loses.
**How to apply:** continue the experiment from "Experiment state"; never cutover launchers without backing up `~/.hermes` first + explicit user approval. If abandoning jcode, `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + delete scratchpad clone.

lines: - Two profiles: `default` → gemma4:12b local (Chip / pocket-llm ext); `openrouter` → agy/Gemini (FreeCode).
- Launchers: `freecode` in `ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`; VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
vscode-launcher) runs `freecode`. The real cutover = repoint these launchers/button from Hermes to jcode.
+- **SCOPE CONSTRAINT (user, 2026-06-25): cutover ONLY the local gemma4 lane** (Hermes `default` profile /

+  freecode / robot button stay on Hermes). jcode replaces ONLY the gemma4-local agent.

content.text:
Risultato chiave: **funziona end-to-end** — gemma4 ha risposto "OK". Ma i `💭` = **thinking ON** (gemma4 ragiona pure su "Say OK"). 65s = load a freddo (8GB→GPU) + reasoning. Questo è il colpevole della lentezza, **identico al trap che Hermes ha già risolto** (reasoning_effort=none → thinking off).

Punto tecnico che decide tutto, prima di scartare:

**Stesso modello + stesso Ollama = stessa velocità token, qualunque harness.** jcode leggero NON fa generare gemma4 più veloce (decode-bound ~7.6 tok/s = pavimento del modello). jcode vince su RAM/boot/UI, **non** su velocità di generazione. Quindi confronto equo = jcode con thinking OFF (come Hermes), warm. Cerco la leva e rifaccio test caldo:

Aggiorno memoria con l'esito e poi ti do il verdetto secco:

input.old_string:
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` (background, long). Next: login ollama + smoke-test gemma4.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.
input.new_string:
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` → OK 4m56s, 94 MB binary (`target/release/jcode`).
- Telemetry: jcode collects anonymous stats by default → opt out with `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (also `JCODE_NO_TELEMETRY`).
- **Smoke-test PASSED** (2026-06-25): `DO_NOT_TRACK=1 jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '...'`
  works end-to-end; `ollama ps` confirms gemma4 loaded 100% GPU, jcode sets num_ctx 16384. No `jcode login`
  needed for ollama (local endpoint, no key).
- **Latency finding**: warm one-liner = **~11s**, cold = ~65s. BUT jcode runs gemma4 with **thinking ON by
  default** (emits `💭` reasoning even for trivial prompts) → that's the slowness, NOT harness weight.
  jcode HAS the lever (`[provider] openai_reasoning_effort` + per-provider `supports_reasoning_effort`,
  provider-core `set_reasoning_effort`) but does NOT send reasoning_effort=none to ollama by default →
  same trap Hermes already fixed (Ollama /v1 honors `reasoning_effort`, ignores `think`). NOT yet tuned in jcode.
- **KEY VERDICT**: same model + same Ollama = same decode floor (~7.6 tok/s) regardless of harness. jcode can
  at best TIE Hermes on gemma4 token speed, never beat it. jcode's real edge = RAM/boot/UI + memory/swarm,
  not generation speed. If the goal is faster gemma4 responses, the lever is a smaller model, not the harness.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.

## mermaid-rs-renderer (separate repo, asked 2026-06-25)
https://github.com/1jehuang/mermaid-rs-renderer — same author, standalone Rust mermaid renderer (no browser/TS,
~1800× faster). Already an OPTIONAL git dep of jcode (`jcode-tui-mermaid` feature `renderer`, tag v0.2.1, default
OFF → our build excluded it). Not needed separately for the jcode eval; only worth adopting standalone if we want
fast mermaid rendering in OTHER tools (Hermes/pocket-llm/a CLI). Noted, not actioned.

---
name: jcode-eval
description: Evaluating jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) as a lighter replacement for Hermes driving local gemma4. In-flight experiment + Hermes rollback baseline.
metadata:
  type: project
---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model
coding harness, driving `gemma4:12b` on Ollama (or a future local model). Hypothesis: jcode is
much lighter/faster (Rust, ~27 MB RAM embedding-off vs Hermes' Python/Node stack), so response
and eval latency should improve. Evaluate REAL impact before committing.

## What jcode is
- Repo: https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode — MIT, single-author (Jeremy Huang), very active.
- Rust coding-agent harness (Codex/Claude-Code category), NOT an LLM. BYO-model: ~40 OpenAI-compatible
  providers + native Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter. Free path = local Ollama / LM Studio / OpenRouter `:free`.
- Ollama profile: api_base `http://localhost:11434/v1`, auth "local endpoint" (no key), default_model none
  → must pass `--model gemma4:12b`. Login: `jcode login --provider ollama` then
  `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '<prompt>'`.
- Differentiators vs Hermes: single-server multi-client daemon (Unix socket, hot-reload), semantic
  memory (local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings + petgraph), swarm (multi-agent same repo), MCP + hooks.
- Maturity caveats: ~277k LOC, **1258 prod `unwrap`/`expect`** (panic risk), 50 files >1200 LOC,
  version mismatch (Cargo 0.31.2 vs badge v0.9.1888-dev), no real stable release, bus factor 1.
  → fine as personal experiment, risky for "prod" reliance.

## Experiment state (2026-06-25)
- Clone: scratchpad temp only (`.../scratchpad/jcode`), NOT in `$WS`. 503 MB.
- Installed **Rust 1.96.0 via Homebrew** (`brew install rust`, pulled `pkgconf` dep) — was absent.
  Removable via `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` if abandoning.
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` (background, long). Next: login ollama + smoke-test gemma4.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.

## Rollback baseline — Hermes (DO NOT switch until eval passes + user OK)
Current Hermes = the thing jcode would replace. See [[hermes-agent-docs]] for full config. Key points:
- Home `~/.hermes`: `config.yaml`, `SOUL.md`, profiles, source checkout `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`.
- Two profiles: `default` → gemma4:12b local (Chip / pocket-llm ext); `openrouter` → agy/Gemini (FreeCode).
- Launchers: `freecode` in `ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`; VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  vscode-launcher) runs `freecode`. The real cutover = repoint these launchers/button from Hermes to jcode.
- **SCOPE CONSTRAINT (user, 2026-06-25): cutover ONLY the local gemma4 lane** (Hermes `default` profile /
  Chip / pocket-llm). The **Free Router lane MUST NOT be touched** (`openrouter` profile / agy/Gemini /
  freecode / robot button stay on Hermes). jcode replaces ONLY the gemma4-local agent.
- Source patch (thinking/temp) in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` via pocket-llm reapply script.
- **Before any cutover**: back up `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (+ profiles) and snapshot launcher files so we
  can revert to Hermes if jcode underperforms.

[[hermes-agent-docs]] [[pocket-llm]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode eval, replace hermes, lighter harness, gemma4 jcode, ollama jcode, rust coding agent, 1jehuang, cutover hermes, rollback hermes
**Why:** user wants to A/B jcode vs Hermes for the local coding lane; needs a clean revert path if jcode loses.
**How to apply:** continue the experiment from "Experiment state"; never cutover launchers without backing up `~/.hermes` first + explicit user approval. If abandoning jcode, `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + delete scratchpad clone.

content.text:
Verdetto eval (onesto, decision-grade):

**Funziona**: jcode → ollama → `gemma4:12b` end-to-end, build OK (94 MB), zero login, telemetria disattivata.

**Velocità — il punto che conta per te**:
- Warm one-liner **11s**, ma con **thinking ON** (gemma4 ragiona pure su banalità). Hermes gira lo stesso gemma4 con **thinking OFF** (tuning che hai già fatto) → là è veloce.
- Confronto attuale = **sleale**: jcode-untuned-thinking-on vs Hermes-tuned-thinking-off. Non è jcode che è lento, è gemma4 che ragiona.
- jcode **ha** la leva (`reasoning_effort`/`set_reasoning_effort`) ma non la manda a ollama di default → stesso trap che Hermes risolse.

**La verità tecnica che decide**: stesso modello + stesso Ollama = **stessa velocità token** (~7.6 tok/s, decode-bound), qualunque harness. jcode può al massimo **pareggiare** Hermes su gemma4, mai batterlo. Il vantaggio jcode è **RAM/boot/UI** + memory/swarm, **non** la generazione. Se l'obiettivo è gemma4 più veloce, la leva è un **modello più piccolo**, non l'harness.

**mermaid-rs-renderer**: già dep opzionale dentro jcode (default off), non serve a parte — utile solo per rendering mermaid veloce in altri tool. Annotato.

**Hermes intatto.** Nessuno switch fatto. Memoria aggiornata + vincolo "solo lane gemma4, mai Free Router" persistito.

Due strade — scegli:
- **(A) Fair fight**: tuno jcode thinking-OFF (config `openai_reasoning_effort=none` + `supports_reasoning_effort`) e rimisuro warm vs Hermes. Se pareggia, valuti se RAM/boot/feature valgono il cambio. ~15-30 min.
- **(B) Scarta ora**: se l'unico metro è "più veloce di Hermes su gemma4", jcode non può vincere (model-bound) e Hermes è già tunato → cleanup (`brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + rimuovo clone scratchpad).

Quale?

---
name: jcode-eval
description: Evaluating jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) as a lighter replacement for Hermes driving local gemma4. In-flight experiment + Hermes rollback baseline.
metadata:
  type: project
---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model
coding harness, driving `gemma4:12b` on Ollama (or a future local model). Hypothesis: jcode is
much lighter/faster (Rust, ~27 MB RAM embedding-off vs Hermes' Python/Node stack), so response
and eval latency should improve. Evaluate REAL impact before committing.

## What jcode is
- Repo: https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode — MIT, single-author (Jeremy Huang), very active.
- Rust coding-agent harness (Codex/Claude-Code category), NOT an LLM. BYO-model: ~40 OpenAI-compatible
  providers + native Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter. Free path = local Ollama / LM Studio / OpenRouter `:free`.
- Ollama profile: api_base `http://localhost:11434/v1`, auth "local endpoint" (no key), default_model none
  → must pass `--model gemma4:12b`. Login: `jcode login --provider ollama` then
  `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '<prompt>'`.
- Differentiators vs Hermes: single-server multi-client daemon (Unix socket, hot-reload), semantic
  memory (local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings + petgraph), swarm (multi-agent same repo), MCP + hooks.
- Maturity caveats: ~277k LOC, **1258 prod `unwrap`/`expect`** (panic risk), 50 files >1200 LOC,
  version mismatch (Cargo 0.31.2 vs badge v0.9.1888-dev), no real stable release, bus factor 1.
  → fine as personal experiment, risky for "prod" reliance.

## Experiment state (2026-06-25)
- Clone: scratchpad temp only (`.../scratchpad/jcode`), NOT in `$WS`. 503 MB.
- Installed **Rust 1.96.0 via Homebrew** (`brew install rust`, pulled `pkgconf` dep) — was absent.
  Removable via `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` if abandoning.
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` → OK 4m56s, 94 MB binary (`target/release/jcode`).
- Telemetry: jcode collects anonymous stats by default → opt out with `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (also `JCODE_NO_TELEMETRY`).
- **Smoke-test PASSED** (2026-06-25): `DO_NOT_TRACK=1 jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '...'`
  works end-to-end; `ollama ps` confirms gemma4 loaded 100% GPU, jcode sets num_ctx 16384. No `jcode login`
  needed for ollama (local endpoint, no key).
- **Latency finding**: warm one-liner = **~11s**, cold = ~65s. BUT jcode runs gemma4 with **thinking ON by
  default** (emits `💭` reasoning even for trivial prompts) → that's the slowness, NOT harness weight.
  jcode HAS the lever (`[provider] openai_reasoning_effort` + per-provider `supports_reasoning_effort`,
  provider-core `set_reasoning_effort`) but does NOT send reasoning_effort=none to ollama by default →
  same trap Hermes already fixed (Ollama /v1 honors `reasoning_effort`, ignores `think`). NOT yet tuned in jcode.
- **KEY VERDICT**: same model + same Ollama = same decode floor (~7.6 tok/s) regardless of harness. jcode can
  at best TIE Hermes on gemma4 token speed, never beat it. jcode's real edge = RAM/boot/UI + memory/swarm,
  not generation speed. If the goal is faster gemma4 responses, the lever is a smaller model, not the harness.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.

## mermaid-rs-renderer (separate repo, asked 2026-06-25)
https://github.com/1jehuang/mermaid-rs-renderer — same author, standalone Rust mermaid renderer (no browser/TS,
~1800× faster). Already an OPTIONAL git dep of jcode (`jcode-tui-mermaid` feature `renderer`, tag v0.2.1, default
OFF → our build excluded it). Not needed separately for the jcode eval; only worth adopting standalone if we want
fast mermaid rendering in OTHER tools (Hermes/pocket-llm/a CLI). Noted, not actioned.

## Rollback baseline — Hermes (DO NOT switch until eval passes + user OK)
Current Hermes = the thing jcode would replace. See [[hermes-agent-docs]] for full config. Key points:
- Home `~/.hermes`: `config.yaml`, `SOUL.md`, profiles, source checkout `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`.
- Two profiles: `default` → gemma4:12b local (Chip / pocket-llm ext); `openrouter` → agy/Gemini (FreeCode).
- Launchers: `freecode` in `ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`; VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  vscode-launcher) runs `freecode`. The real cutover = repoint these launchers/button from Hermes to jcode.
- **SCOPE CONSTRAINT (user, 2026-06-25): cutover ONLY the local gemma4 lane** (Hermes `default` profile /
  Chip / pocket-llm). The **Free Router lane MUST NOT be touched** (`openrouter` profile / agy/Gemini /
  freecode / robot button stay on Hermes). jcode replaces ONLY the gemma4-local agent.
- Source patch (thinking/temp) in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` via pocket-llm reapply script.
- **Before any cutover**: back up `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (+ profiles) and snapshot launcher files so we
  can revert to Hermes if jcode underperforms.

[[hermes-agent-docs]] [[pocket-llm]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode eval, replace hermes, lighter harness, gemma4 jcode, ollama jcode, rust coding agent, 1jehuang, cutover hermes, rollback hermes
**Why:** user wants to A/B jcode vs Hermes for the local coding lane; needs a clean revert path if jcode loses.
**How to apply:** continue the experiment from "Experiment state"; never cutover launchers without backing up `~/.hermes` first + explicit user approval. If abandoning jcode, `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + delete scratchpad clone.

content.text:
Tunato e rimisurato. Esito netto:

**Thinking-off tunato → warm 8s** (da 11s), zero reasoning, output corretto. Leva confermata. Ma 8s in one-shot `jcode run` paga ogni volta server-connect + prompt-eval; in TUI interattiva (server caldo persistente) sarebbe più scattante.

**Il verdetto reale**: tunato, **jcode ≈ Hermes** su gemma4 — pareggia, non vince. Il pavimento è gemma4 (~7.6 tok/s decode-bound), identico sotto qualunque harness. Quindi **la scelta NON è sulla velocità** (lì sono pari), ma sugli altri vantaggi jcode:
- ✅ RAM ~27 MB vs stack Python/Node di Hermes; boot/UI più reattivi; memory semantica + swarm.
- ❌ Hermes è maturo e **già tunato** (thinking-off, temp 0.6, profili, launcher, patch auto-riapplicata). jcode = single-author, 1258 unwrap, da tunare/cablare ogni leva da zero.

Se cerchi gemma4 **più veloce**, né jcode né Hermes lo danno — serve un modello più piccolo, non un harness diverso.

Hermes intatto. Memoria aggiornata+pushata.

Decisione, scegli:
- **(A) Adotta jcode sulla lane gemma4**: persisto thinking-off in config (`extra_body`), poi ripunto SOLO il launcher gemma4 (pocket-llm/Chip) su jcode, con backup `~/.hermes` prima. Free Router intatto.
- **(B) Coexistence**: tengo jcode buildato come alternativa provabile, Hermes resta primario, decidi più avanti.
- **(C) Scarta**: cleanup `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + rimuovo clone scratchpad.

content.text:
Funziona — gemma4 ha risposto ("Ciao. How can I help you today?"). Il **55s = load a freddo** + un check che è andato in **timeout** → jcode ha aperto il pannello setup Ollama che chiede una API key.

**Cosa fare ORA, al prompt `2>`:**
- **Premi Enter** (skip). Ollama non vuole chiave — non incollare niente. Il `api-key:ollama` in alto è solo un placeholder.

Poi:
- Richatta (es. "scrivi funzione python che inverte stringa") → la 2ª risposta scende a ~8s (modello caldo). Il "timed out" era solo la prima a freddo che ha sforato il check.
- Se ti chiede ancora la key, `/cancel` chiude il pannello e resti in chat.

Cose da notare nel test:
- Risponde in **inglese** (jcode non ha persona italiana come Hermes via SOUL/HERMES.md). Se adotti, gli do un `AGENTS.md` con la tua persona IT.
- Pannello mostra **ctx 200k** (default generico) — gemma4 reale = 262k, ma Ollama ne carica 16384 a runtime. Ininfluente.
- Prova: `/model`, side panel, mermaid inline, multi-turn.

Se il primo-token a freddo ti dà fastidio (il timeout iniziale), posso alzare `JCODE_STREAM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECS` nel launcher così non ti apre più il pannello setup. Dimmi com'è il giro e se vuoi quel fix.

content.text:
Keybind paste su macOS = **Cmd+V**, e jcode legge la clipboard (testo o immagine via `osascript`). Problema tipico: **il terminale macOS intercetta Cmd+V** e incolla solo testo (bracketed-paste) prima che jcode legga l'immagine → l'immagine si perde. Non è gemma4 (ha vision), è il terminale.

Per isolare: verifico se `jcode run` accetta un'immagine headless, così provo la vision bypassando il paste-terminale:

content.text: `run` non ha flag immagine → input immagini è solo TUI/clipboard. Tu hai messo uno screenshot fresco in clipboard e Cmd+V non l'ha preso. Due sospetti: terminale che ruba Cmd+V, o l'estrazione `osascript` di jcode che fallisce su macOS. Leggo il comando macOS esatto che jcode usa per replicarlo e capire dove si rompe:

Il read macOS di jcode usa AppleScript NSPasteboard → PNG. Testo se funziona su questa macchina: metto un'immagine in clipboard e lancio lo STESSO script di jcode. Se estrae → il read va, colpa del terminale che ruba Cmd+V:

else

content.text: Errore è mio quoting inline, non di jcode. Riscrivo lo script su file e rilancio pulito:

input.command:
cat > /tmp/jcode_extract.applescript <<'EOF'
use framework "AppKit"
set pb to current application's NSPasteboard's generalPasteboard()
set imgClasses to current application's NSArray's arrayWithObject:(current application's NSImage)
if (pb's canReadObjectForClasses:imgClasses options:(missing value)) then
	set imgList to pb's readObjectsForClasses:imgClasses options:(missing value)
	set img to item 1 of imgList
	set tiffData to img's TIFFRepresentation()
	set bitmapRep to current application's NSBitmapImageRep's imageRepWithData:tiffData
	set pngData to bitmapRep's representationUsingType:(current application's NSBitmapImageFileTypePNG) properties:(missing value)
	pngData's writeToFile:"/tmp/jcode_clipboard.png" atomically:true
	return "ok"
else
	return "none"
end if
EOF
rm -f /tmp/jcode_clipboard.png; echo "=== run ==="; osascript -l AppleScript /tmp/jcode_extract.applescript 2>&1; echo "=== result file ==="; ls -lh /tmp/jcode_clipboard.png 2>&1 | head -1; file /tmp/jcode_clipboard.png 2>&1
