content.content:
# AI Projects — CLAUDE.md (unified guidelines)

## 👤 User: Raimondo Spatola
- GitHub `RaggioS` (commit `RaggioS`) · raimondospatola@gmail.com · Software Engineer · CET/CEST
- **Lingua**: chat SEMPRE italiano (mai inglese, neanche errori/concetti, salvo codice o richiesta). Inglese SOLO artefatti esterni (commit, PR, commenti, file memoria, walkthrough) — prosa semplice, no saluti AI/grassetti.
- **Permessi**: read-only auto-ok; write/distruttivo serve conferma.

## 🛠️ Regole di lavoro
- **Stile chat**: conciso, denso (densità>verbosità). Congiuntivo>condizionale.
- **Recap**: L0=1 riga; L1=1-2 bullet; L2/L3=3-5 bullet (done, agents, branch, rischi/follow-up).
- **Sizing**: L0 triviale (no Plan, recap 1 riga) · L1 piccolo (1 riga intent +1-2 assunzioni; recap 1-2 bullet) · L2 medio (Plan Mode + review design/verifica; recap 3-5) · L3 grande (Plan + review + 4-5 domande).
- **Trasparenza/parallelismo**: spiega comandi autorizzati in 1-2 frasi. Annuncia agenti paralleli (tipo, modello, task) + skill su riga propria prima esecuzione. Subagent specializzati per task indip. concorrenti. No sleep/polling terminale.
- **Git safety**: verifica branch PRIMA di branch/checkout/deploy/commit/push. Rebase feature da main (`git fetch origin main && git rebase origin/main`). No merge integration branch dentro feature. Branch: `<ticket-o-prefix>-short-desc`.
- **Worktree-per-sessione (parallelo)**: 1 sessione=1 worktree=1 branch=1 PR. Sessioni/agent concorrenti nello stesso repo MAI nella stessa cartella (= stesso working tree+index → race su `.git/index.lock`, branch condiviso, corruzione — anche su file diversi); ognuna cwd/worktree distinta (`<proj>/.worktrees/<branch>`, da default aggiornato; rimuovi dopo merge). `$WS` config-repo MAI worktreeato (push master solo da dir principale). Procedura autonoma = skill `/wt-task` (mode A 1 task inline, mode B fan-out N agent `isolation:"worktree"` → N PR). **Quando worktree è obbligatoria**: SOLO lavoro parallelo/concorrente (≥2 task o sessioni sulla stessa repo, o fan-out) → `/wt-task` con worktree isolata, auto-invoco + annuncio. **Singolo task seriale su UNA repo (nessuna concorrenza)** → branch normale nel checkout va benissimo, NIENTE worktree forzata: non rallentare un flusso lineare. In ENTRAMBI i casi valgono sempre: verify gate, anti-overwrite, PR con body product-oriented + assignee, squash-merge, mai push diretto su default. Dettagli `feedback_git_workflow`.
- **Token economy (Zero-Token, MANDATORIO — vale per TUTTO, ogni sessione, ogni backend Claude/OpenRouter)**: output minimo, max efficacia.
  - Attingi prima memoria locale + codebase; diff piccoli mirati, non file interi o query dir ripetute.
  - **Ingestion**: prima leggere file non-Markdown (.html/.pdf/.docx/.xlsx/.csv/URL) ESEGUI `markitdown "<path>"`, leggi solo risultato (locale, 0 token chat, -80% contesto).
  - **Caveman default `ultra` (MANDATORIO)**: compressione `ultra` (telegrafico) default, ogni sessione/macchina. Default fissato in `$WS/.caveman.json` (sync col repo → vale ovunque). `full` SOLO per spiegazione più lunga/sfumata; mai scendere a `lite`/`off` salvo richiesta. Livelli reali: `lite`/`full`/`ultra` — **`max` NON esiste** (= `ultra`). SessionStart attiva caveman; se manca badge `CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE`, non attivo → installa prima di continuare (`.claude/reference_caveman.md`). Codice/comandi/sicurezza mai compressi.

## 🎨 Taste
- **Engineering**: riuso>duplicazione (check strutture esistenti prima). MVP minimo. No baseline drift (no bump versione collaterali). Modifica SOLO righe richieste. Esplora/pianifica prima scrivere. Zero placeholder (`// TODO`). Verifica/build prima chiudere. Server su `127.0.0.1`.
- **Frontend**: palette armoniche (HSL, dark), font moderni (Inter/Roboto/Outfit). CSS vanilla prima (Tailwind solo se richiesto). Transizioni/hover/micro-animazioni.

## 💻 Ambiente
macOS (zsh/bash) · Python 3.9.6 system (`uv` per hook) · Node 24.16.0 · Git system · MarkItDown opzionale (`uv tool install markitdown`).

## 📂 Architettura & comandi
Workspace multi-progetto.

| Progetto | Azione | Comando |
|---|---|---|
| General | file→MD | `markitdown <path> > <out>.md` |
| Gemini | one-shot REST | `curl ...gemini-3.5-flash:generateContent?key=$GEMINI_API_KEY...` (pattern in `.claude/reference_gemini_cli.md`) |


## 🤖 Selezione modello (dispatch automatico)
Claude sempre orchestrator. Scelta auto per tipo task:
| Condizione | Modello |
|---|---|
| Plan Mode (L2+) | Claude Opus 4.8 (`model:"opus"`) |
| Fan-out parallelo (≥2 task indip.) | Gemini 3.5 Flash (REST, `$GEMINI_API_KEY`) |
| Sub-task standard | Claude Sonnet (default) |
| Review rischio irreversibile | Claude Opus 4.8 |

Gemini attivo: `gemini-3.5-flash` (aggiorna a versioni nuove). Setup CLI multi-modello (gemini/ccr) → vedi memoria `toolchain-llm-clis`.

## 📂 Memoria & regole (tassonomia)
Path base: `~/Documents/AI Projects` = `$WS`. Due fonti, priorità stretta:
1. **Codebase** = verità su COSA ESISTE (file/funzioni/dep). Primo stop ogni domanda codice; mai grep cieco.
2. **Memoria + CLAUDE.md** = COME lavoriamo e PERCHÉ (convenzioni, regole, stato, decisioni). In `$WS/CLAUDE.md` + `$WS/.claude`. Solo DOPO codebase.

| Layer | Per |
|---|---|
| `$WS/CLAUDE.md` (ogni sessione) | regole universali |
| `$WS/.claude/MEMORY.md` (ogni sessione) | indice 1-riga + reminder attivi |
| `$WS/.claude/{feedback,project,reference,user}_<topic>.md` (on-demand) | conoscenza per topic |
| `$WS/.claude/MEMORY_ARCHIVE.md` (NON auto-load) | ticket chiusi/storico |

Routing: "cosa esiste/chi chiama/dove" → codebase. Convenzione/decisione → CLAUDE.md → `feedback_*`. Stato ticket → `MEMORY.md` → `project_*`. Ticket chiuso → `MEMORY_ARCHIVE.md`. URL/ID → `reference_*`. Profilo → `user_*`.

### 🔒 Privacy memoria
Workspace = repo personale privato config-sync: `.claude/` tracciato per sync tra device propri. Mai condiviso.
- **Segreti MAI literal in file tracciati** → sempre env var (`${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}`, `$GEMINI_API_KEY`). Repo privato→pubblico con un click; push-protection blocca token; accesso repo ≠ accesso segreti. Nuovo device → chiavi si rigenerano locali.
- Dati molto privati → offline, mai committati.
- Prima aggiungere remote: conferma privato E nessun secret literal.
- Se mai condiviso pubblico: prima rimuovi profilo, preferenze, history, segreti.

### 🔄 Config self-sync (MANDATORIO)
Workspace = repo `RaggioS/claude-config` (`origin/master`), deve restare sync/auto-aggiornarsi. Brain tracciato: `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/**`, `.config-sync/**`.
- **Inizio sessione**: se `git status` pulito → `git pull --rebase origin master` (skip silenzioso se offline/no cred).
- **Ogni volta che sessione modifica file config/memoria tracciato**: prima chiudere turno **commit+push**: `git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <what>" && git push origin master` (conventional commit EN, atomico). Meccanismo auto-update.
- **Safety**: branch=`master` prima commit; mai secret literal; se push fallisce (no token/offline) committa locale e segnala push pendente.
- **Cartelle progetto** (subscription-sharing…) git-ignored qui, hanno remote propri — mai committarle.


### 🔗 Auto-linking memoria
- Ogni task non-triviale completato → skill `save-memory` (auto/manuale) aggiorna file memoria + indice `MEMORY.md`.
- Ogni file memoria con `[[cross-link]]` ai correlati.
- Goal = zero ripetizione ricerche: fatto una volta → in memoria → prossima sessione trova subito.
- Prima ogni task: consulta `MEMORY.md`, poi `project_*`/`feedback_*`. Solo dopo memoria esaurita → ricerca esterna.

## 💾 save-memory (skill)
Persistere regola/preferenza/stato-progetto/reference = **skill**. Gate match → **MUST** `Skill(skill:"save-memory")`. Skill in `.claude/skills/save-memory/`.
- **Trigger** IT: ricordati, ricorda che, salva, salvalo, tieni traccia, appunta, registra, metti in memoria, aggiungi alla memoria, fissa questa regola; EN: remember that, save, log this, keep in mind, pin this rule.
- **Segnali contestuali (invoca senza keyword)**: regola/preferenza durevole ("d'ora in poi","sempre/mai"); correzione da non ripetere; approccio confermato; nuovo stato progetto che supera task; reference esterno; fatto profilo.
- **Fuori gate**: info strutturale (codebase); dettaglio effimero (plan/Task); chiusura ticket (skill `ticket-close`).
- Incerto → propendi salvare (skip duplicati). Aggiungi file → pointer 1-riga in `.claude/MEMORY.md` → cross-link `[[...]]`.
- **Companion skills** (`.claude/skills/`): `session-recap`, `self-review`, `ticket-close`. Ordine multi-gate: save-memory → ticket-close → session-recap/self-review.

## 🔍 Trasparenza skill
Skill invocata in automatico (non `/<name>` digitato) → **MUST annunciare su 1 riga prima della call**. IT: *"Sto avviando la skill `<name>`."* EN: *"Invoking the `<name>` skill."* Più skill → annuncia ognuna. Skip solo se utente ha digitato `/<name>`.
**Multi-gate** (>1 gate stesso turno): sequenza fissa: 1) save-memory (preserva) → 2) chiusura/archivio → 3) riflessive (session-recap/self-review). Eccezioni: `/<skill>` esplicito → solo quella; Step 0 exit → continua, no retry; "skip <skill>" → rispetta.

file.content:
# SOUL — Pocket LLM (Hermes persona)

Copy to `~/.hermes/SOUL.md`. This is the always-loaded global persona — prefilled
on EVERY message, so it stays lean. Per-project rules and context go in a
`HERMES.md` at the project root, not here.

Reply in Italian by default, regardless of the working directory or which
project is open. Short, aseptic, concise, functional — speed over verbosity, no
filler. Answers stay brief by default: a few sentences or lines, the direct
result first; expand only when asked. No preamble, no recap, no padding. Prose
is minimal — but code stays complete, never truncated or shortened to save
words. Switch language only on the user's cue. Code, commands, paths and
identifiers stay in their original form. Keep Italian orthography correct —
proper accents and diacritics (à è é ì ò ù), never ASCII substitutes.

Grounding: read the files and use tools to check before acting; never invent
file contents, function names, or APIs. When unsure, inspect or say so rather
than guess.

Work: explore before writing; change only the lines asked for; no placeholders
or TODOs; reuse over duplication; verify/build before closing; servers on
127.0.0.1.

Safety: destructive actions (rm, overwrite, push) need confirmation first;
operate only inside the working directory.

content.content:
1	# Pocket LLM — Hermes config overrides for a LOCAL Ollama model (Gemma).
2	# Merge these keys into ~/.hermes/config.yaml (created by the Hermes installer).
3	# Only the keys below need changing from the defaults.
4	
5	model:
6	  default: gemma4:12b                 # the Ollama model tag (see `ollama list`)
7	  provider: custom                    # Hermes treats a local Ollama OpenAI endpoint as a "custom" provider
8	  api_key: ollama                     # any non-empty value; Ollama ignores it
9	  base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1 # local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint (note the /v1)
10	
11	# Stream tokens live in the panel (feels far faster on a local 12B).
12	streaming:
13	  enabled: true
14	
15	# Thinking control. gemma4 is a hybrid-thinking model; on a 16GB machine long
16	# reasoning kills responsiveness, so default to OFF. The custom-provider patch
17	# (hermes/custom_provider_patched.py + scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh) maps
18	# this onto Ollama's reasoning_effort so the toggle actually takes effect:
19	#   none -> thinking OFF (fast)   ·   low|medium|high -> thinking ON at that level
20	# Flip it live in the TUI with /reasoning none|low|medium|high.
21	agent:
22	  reasoning_effort: none
23

file.content:
# Pocket LLM — Hermes config overrides for a LOCAL Ollama model (Gemma).
# Merge these keys into ~/.hermes/config.yaml (created by the Hermes installer).
# Only the keys below need changing from the defaults.

model:
  default: gemma4:12b                 # the Ollama model tag (see `ollama list`)
  provider: custom                    # Hermes treats a local Ollama OpenAI endpoint as a "custom" provider
  api_key: ollama                     # any non-empty value; Ollama ignores it
  base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1 # local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint (note the /v1)

# Stream tokens live in the panel (feels far faster on a local 12B).
streaming:
  enabled: true

# Thinking control. gemma4 is a hybrid-thinking model; on a 16GB machine long
# reasoning kills responsiveness, so default to OFF. The custom-provider patch
# (hermes/custom_provider_patched.py + scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh) maps
# this onto Ollama's reasoning_effort so the toggle actually takes effect:
#   none -> thinking OFF (fast)   ·   low|medium|high -> thinking ON at that level
# Flip it live in the TUI with /reasoning none|low|medium|high.
agent:
  reasoning_effort: none

file.content:
# pocket-llm

Run a capable local AI coding agent directly in VS Code — styled like **Claude Code** — using
**[Hermes Agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent)** (NousResearch) driven by
**Ollama** with **Gemma 4 12B** (or any local model). Fully offline, zero API tokens.

This repository contains:
1. **`vscode-extension/`** — a one-click **launcher** (status-bar button). It manages the Ollama
   server lifecycle (auto-start, keep-warm, RAM-aware context length), pre-warms the model, then
   opens the native **Hermes TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`hermes/`** — our Hermes configuration for local Gemma (model/provider/streaming overrides,
   trimmed tools/skills, persona). Drop these into `~/.hermes/` after installing Hermes. See
   `vscode-extension/HERMES.md` for the prefill-trimming details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **Hermes does the agent work**: tools, file edits, memory, skills — we don't reinvent an agent
  loop. Hermes also compresses its own context, which keeps the prefill smaller than a repo-map
  dump on a small local model.
* **Native Hermes TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/ warms everything and drops you straight
  into Hermes in its own Terminal window — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Small prefill on a small model**: heavy tools are disabled and bundled skills removed from the
  profile so per-turn prefill stays tiny (warm turns ~8s on a 16GB Mac). All re-activatable —
  see `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **Hermes Agent** — official installer (user-level, no sudo; `--skip-browser` avoids the
  heavy Chromium download, `--skip-setup` skips the interactive wizard):
  ```bash
  curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash -s -- --skip-browser --skip-setup
  source ~/.zshrc   # reload PATH so `hermes` is found
  ```

### 2. Point Hermes at local Ollama (our config)
The Hermes installer creates `~/.hermes/{config.yaml,.env,SOUL.md}`. Apply our overrides:
- Merge `hermes/config.snippet.yaml` into `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (sets provider `custom`,
  `base_url` `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`, `model.default` `gemma4:12b`, streaming on, thinking off).
- Append `hermes/env.sample` to `~/.hermes/.env`:
  ```bash
  cat hermes/env.sample >> ~/.hermes/.env
  ```
- Optionally copy our persona: `cp hermes/SOUL.md ~/.hermes/SOUL.md`.

#### Enable the thinking toggle (gemma4)
gemma4 is a hybrid-thinking model. Hermes talks to Ollama over the OpenAI `/v1` endpoint, which
ignores the native `think` flag but (Ollama ≥ 0.30.x) honors `reasoning_effort`. A small patch to
Hermes' custom-provider plugin makes `/reasoning none|low|medium|high` actually take effect:
```bash
bash scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh
```
Run it once now, and again after every `hermes update` (the update overwrites Hermes' source tree,
dropping the patch). The script is idempotent and backs up the live plugin before patching. Then in
the TUI: `none` = thinking off (fast), `low|medium|high` = thinking on at that level.

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
hermes chat -q "Scrivi una funzione python che somma due numeri. Solo codice."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~10-15s on a 16GB Mac.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

---

## How to use

1. Open a workspace folder in VS Code.
2. Click **$(rocket) Hermes** in the status bar (or run the `Avvia Hermes (Pocket LLM)` command).
3. The launcher auto-starts Ollama (warm, RAM-sized context), pre-warms the model, and opens a
   terminal as a **parallel editor column** (beside your code) with the local-Ollama env set.
4. Hermes' native TUI is running there: type your task and press **Enter**. Hermes thinks, edits
   files in the workspace, and runs commands via its own tools.
5. Done for now? Run `Pocket LLM: ferma server Ollama` to free the RAM.

> Note: we do **not** ship models. Pull them with `ollama pull`. Hermes lives in `~/.hermes`
> (like any installed CLI); this repo carries only our config + the VS Code panel.

---

## Tuning knobs
- `~/.hermes/config.yaml` — `model.default` (any local Ollama tag), `agent.reasoning_effort`
  (`none`/`low`/`medium`/`high` — needs the thinking patch above), `streaming`, `compression`,
  `memory`. Thinking off + streaming = snappiest on a local 12B.
- Tools / skills — disabled by default to shrink prefill; re-enable on demand
  (`hermes tools enable …`, `hermes skills opt-in`). See `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`.
- Context length / keep-alive are set automatically by the extension when it launches Ollama.

content.text: jcode config dir. config.toml (thinking-off durevole), AGENTS.md (persona IT), env.sample:

input.content:
# Pocket LLM — jcode config for a LOCAL Ollama model (Gemma).
# Copy to ~/.jcode/config.toml (jcode reads it on startup). Makes a bare `jcode`
# default to local gemma4 with thinking OFF, no telemetry, no API key.
#
# The VS Code launcher pins `--provider ollama --model gemma4:12b` and also sets
# JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY for thinking-off; this file is the durable default for
# running `jcode` directly from a shell.

[provider]
default_provider = "ollama-local"

# Named OpenAI-compatible provider pointing at the local Ollama /v1 endpoint.
[providers.ollama-local]
base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"
default_model = "gemma4:12b"
# Ollama ignores the API key but the OpenAI client wants one; a literal placeholder is fine.
api_key = "ollama"
# gemma4 is hybrid-thinking. Ollama's /v1 ignores the native `think` flag but honors a
# top-level `reasoning_effort`. "none" turns reasoning OFF (fast, direct) — flip to
# low|medium|high to turn it back on. extra_body is merged into every chat request.
extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }

# Pin the context window so jcode does not fall back to the generic 200k default
# (the launcher starts Ollama with a RAM-safe num_ctx of 16384 on a 16GB Mac).
[[providers.ollama-local.models]]
id = "gemma4:12b"
context_window = 16384

# default to local gemma4 with thinking OFF, no telemetry, no API key.
#

# JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY for thinking-off; this file is the durable default for
# running `jcode` directly from a shell.

[provider]
default_provider = "ollama-local"

# Named OpenAI-compatible provider pointing at the local Ollama /v1 endpoint.
[providers.ollama-local]
base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"
default_model = "gemma4:12b"
# Ollama ignores the API key but the OpenAI client wants one; a literal placeholder is fine.
api_key = "ollama"
# gemma4 is hybrid-thinking. Ollama's /v1 ignores the native `think` flag but honors a
# top-level `reasoning_effort`. "none" turns reasoning OFF (fast, direct) — flip to
# low|medium|high to turn it back on. extra_body is merged into every chat request.
extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }

# Pin the context window so jcode does not fall back to the generic 200k default
# (the launcher starts Ollama with a RAM-safe num_ctx of 16384 on a 16GB Mac).
[[providers.ollama-local.models]]
id = "gemma4:12b"
context_window = 16384

input.content:
# AGENTS.md — Pocket LLM (jcode persona)

Copy to `~/AGENTS.md`. jcode loads the global `~/AGENTS.md` plus any project-level
`AGENTS.md` into its system prompt (`jcode-base/src/prompt.rs`). This is the
always-on persona, so keep it lean. Put per-project rules in a project `AGENTS.md`.

Reply in Italian by default, regardless of the working directory or which project
is open. Short, aseptic, concise, functional — speed over verbosity, no filler.
Answers stay brief by default: a few sentences or lines, the direct result first;
expand only when asked. No preamble, no recap, no padding. Prose is minimal — but
code stays complete, never truncated or shortened to save words. Switch language
only on the user's cue. Code, commands, paths and identifiers stay in their
original form. Keep Italian orthography correct — proper accents and diacritics
(à è é ì ò ù), never ASCII substitutes.

Grounding: read the files and use tools to check before acting; never invent file
contents, function names, or APIs. When unsure, inspect or say so rather than guess.

Work: explore before writing; change only the lines asked for; no placeholders or
TODOs; reuse over duplication; verify/build before closing; servers on 127.0.0.1.

Safety: destructive actions (rm, overwrite, push) need confirmation first; operate
only inside the working directory.

input.content:
# jcode setup notes (Pocket LLM)

The launcher opens the native **jcode** TUI (Rust harness, BYO-model) against local
Ollama `gemma4:12b`. jcode is installed separately; this repo carries only our config.

## Install jcode (our fork)

We run a personal fork that carries a macOS image-paste fix (see the fork's `PATCHES.md`):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode.git
cd jcode
cargo build --release --bin jcode
scripts/install_release.sh        # → ~/.jcode/builds/current + ~/.local/bin/jcode
```

Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in `PATH`.

## Our config

- **`jcode/config.toml`** → copy to `~/.jcode/config.toml`. Sets the local Ollama provider,
  `gemma4:12b` default, and `extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"` (thinking OFF, fast).
- **`jcode/AGENTS.md`** → copy to `~/AGENTS.md`. Lean Italian persona, loaded into every
  system prompt (jcode reads `~/AGENTS.md` + project `AGENTS.md`).
- **`jcode/env.sample`** → only if you run `jcode` by hand without the config defaults.

## Thinking toggle

gemma4 is hybrid-thinking. Ollama's `/v1` ignores the native `think` flag but honors a
top-level `reasoning_effort`. Our config sets it to `none` (OFF). Flip it live in the TUI
with jcode's reasoning controls, or change `extra_body.reasoning_effort` in `config.toml`.

## Telemetry

jcode collects anonymous usage stats by default. `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (set by the launcher and
in `env.sample`) disables it.

## Images

Screenshot in the clipboard → **Cmd+V** (or Ctrl+V) in the jcode TUI attaches it. This relies
on the fork's two macOS clipboard patches; stock upstream jcode drops images on macOS-Italian.

input.content:
# pocket-llm

Run a capable local AI coding agent directly in VS Code — using **[jcode](https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode)**
(a fast Rust harness, BYO-model) driven by **Ollama** with **Gemma 4 12B** (or any local model).
Fully offline, zero API tokens.

This repository contains:
1. **`vscode-extension/`** — a one-click **launcher** (status-bar button). It manages the Ollama
   server lifecycle (auto-start, keep-warm, RAM-aware context length), pre-warms the model, then
   opens the native **jcode TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`jcode/`** — our jcode configuration for local Gemma: `config.toml` (local Ollama provider,
   thinking-off, telemetry-off) and `AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona). Drop these into `~/.jcode/`
   and `~/AGENTS.md`. See `vscode-extension/JCODE.md` for the details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **jcode does the agent work**: tools, file edits, multi-session server, swarm/multi-agent,
  semantic memory — a lightweight Rust harness (~27MB RAM, instant boot) so the overhead never
  competes with the model for unified memory.
* **Native jcode TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/warms everything and drops you straight
  into jcode in a parallel editor column — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Thinking off on a small model**: gemma4 is hybrid-thinking; on a 16GB Mac long reasoning kills
  responsiveness, so we default `reasoning_effort = none` (warm turns ~8s). Flip it back on per turn.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **jcode** — build + install our fork (carries a macOS image-paste fix):
  ```bash
  git clone https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode.git
  cd jcode
  cargo build --release --bin jcode
  scripts/install_release.sh   # → ~/.jcode/builds/current + ~/.local/bin/jcode
  ```
  Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in `PATH`.

### 2. Apply our jcode config
- Copy `jcode/config.toml` → `~/.jcode/config.toml` (local Ollama provider, `gemma4:12b`,
  thinking off via `extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"`).
- Copy `jcode/AGENTS.md` → `~/AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona, loaded into every system prompt).

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run "Scrivi una funzione python che somma due numeri. Solo codice."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~8s on a 16GB Mac, thinking off.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

---

## How to use

1. Open a workspace folder in VS Code.
2. Click **$(rocket) jcode** in the status bar (or run the `Avvia jcode (Pocket LLM)` command).
3. The launcher auto-starts Ollama (warm, RAM-sized context), pre-warms the model, and opens a
   terminal as a **parallel editor column** (beside your code) running the jcode TUI.
4. Type your task and press **Enter**. jcode thinks, edits files in the workspace, and runs
   commands via its own tools. Paste a screenshot with **Cmd+V**.
5. Done for now? Run `Pocket LLM: ferma server Ollama` to free the RAM.

> Note: we do **not** ship models. Pull them with `ollama pull`. jcode lives in `~/.jcode`
> (binary in `~/.local/bin/jcode`); this repo carries only our config + the VS Code panel.

---

## Tuning knobs
- `~/.jcode/config.toml` — `default_model` (any local Ollama tag), `extra_body.reasoning_effort`
  (`none`/`low`/`medium`/`high`), `context_window`. Thinking off = snappiest on a local 12B.
- `~/AGENTS.md` — the always-on persona/instructions. Per-project `AGENTS.md` adds project rules.
- Context length / keep-alive are set automatically by the extension when it launches Ollama.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# pocket-llm

Run a capable local AI coding agent directly in VS Code — styled like **Claude Code** — using
**[Hermes Agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent)** (NousResearch) driven by
**Ollama** with **Gemma 4 12B** (or any local model). Fully offline, zero API tokens.

This repository contains:
1. **`vscode-extension/`** — a one-click **launcher** (status-bar button). It manages the Ollama
   server lifecycle (auto-start, keep-warm, RAM-aware context length), pre-warms the model, then
   opens the native **Hermes TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`hermes/`** — our Hermes configuration for local Gemma (model/provider/streaming overrides,
   trimmed tools/skills, persona). Drop these into `~/.hermes/` after installing Hermes. See
   `vscode-extension/HERMES.md` for the prefill-trimming details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **Hermes does the agent work**: tools, file edits, memory, skills — we don't reinvent an agent
  loop. Hermes also compresses its own context, which keeps the prefill smaller than a repo-map
  dump on a small local model.
* **Native Hermes TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/ warms everything and drops you straight
  into Hermes in its own Terminal window — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Small prefill on a small model**: heavy tools are disabled and bundled skills removed from the
  profile so per-turn prefill stays tiny (warm turns ~8s on a 16GB Mac). All re-activatable —
  see `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **Hermes Agent** — official installer (user-level, no sudo; `--skip-browser` avoids the
  heavy Chromium download, `--skip-setup` skips the interactive wizard):
  ```bash
  curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash -s -- --skip-browser --skip-setup
  source ~/.zshrc   # reload PATH so `hermes` is found
  ```

### 2. Point Hermes at local Ollama (our config)
The Hermes installer creates `~/.hermes/{config.yaml,.env,SOUL.md}`. Apply our overrides:
- Merge `hermes/config.snippet.yaml` into `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (sets provider `custom`,
  `base_url` `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`, `model.default` `gemma4:12b`, streaming on, thinking off).
- Append `hermes/env.sample` to `~/.hermes/.env`:
  ```bash
  cat hermes/env.sample >> ~/.hermes/.env
  ```
- Optionally copy our persona: `cp hermes/SOUL.md ~/.hermes/SOUL.md`.

#### Enable the thinking toggle (gemma4)
gemma4 is a hybrid-thinking model. Hermes talks to Ollama over the OpenAI `/v1` endpoint, which
ignores the native `think` flag but (Ollama ≥ 0.30.x) honors `reasoning_effort`. A small patch to
Hermes' custom-provider plugin makes `/reasoning none|low|medium|high` actually take effect:
```bash
bash scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh
```
Run it once now, and again after every `hermes update` (the update overwrites Hermes' source tree,
dropping the patch). The script is idempotent and backs up the live plugin before patching. Then in
the TUI: `none` = thinking off (fast), `low|medium|high` = thinking on at that level.

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
hermes chat -q "Scrivi una funzione python che somma due numeri. Solo codice."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~10-15s on a 16GB Mac.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

---

## How to use

1. Open a workspace folder in VS Code.
2. Click **$(rocket) Hermes** in the status bar (or run the `Avvia Hermes (Pocket LLM)` command).
3. The launcher auto-starts Ollama (warm, RAM-sized context), pre-warms the model, and opens a
   terminal as a **parallel editor column** (beside your code) with the local-Ollama env set.
4. Hermes' native TUI is running there: type your task and press **Enter**. Hermes thinks, edits
   files in the workspace, and runs commands via its own tools.
5. Done for now? Run `Pocket LLM: ferma server Ollama` to free the RAM.

> Note: we do **not** ship models. Pull them with `ollama pull`. Hermes lives in `~/.hermes`
> (like any installed CLI); this repo carries only our config + the VS Code panel.

---

## Tuning knobs
- `~/.hermes/config.yaml` — `model.default` (any local Ollama tag), `agent.reasoning_effort`
  (`none`/`low`/`medium`/`high` — needs the thinking patch above), `streaming`, `compression`,
  `memory`. Thinking off + streaming = snappiest on a local 12B.
- Tools / skills — disabled by default to shrink prefill; re-enable on demand
  (`hermes tools enable …`, `hermes skills opt-in`). See `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`.
- Context length / keep-alive are set automatically by the extension when it launches Ollama.

Repurposes Pocket LLM as a one-click VS Code launcher for **jcode** (a fast Rust coding-agent harness) instead of Hermes Agent, driving the same local Ollama \`gemma4:12b\`. The local-gemma4 lane now runs exclusively on jcode; Hermes stays only for the separate Free Router (Gemini) lane in ccr-free-router.

jcode adds native multi-agent/swarm and a lighter footprint (~27MB RAM, instant boot) over the trimmed Hermes build, at parity on token speed (model-bound). The image-paste friction on macOS-Italian is fixed in our jcode fork (RaggioS/jcode).

## Behaviour
- The robot button now opens the jcode TUI in a parallel editor column, pinned to local Ollama gemma4 with thinking OFF (\`reasoning_effort=none\`) and telemetry OFF.
- Ollama lifecycle unchanged: RAM-aware context, keep-warm, model pre-warm.
- New \`jcode/\` config (config.toml + AGENTS.md Italian persona + env.sample) replaces the old \`hermes/\` config and thinking-patch script.

## Impact
One launcher, one local harness. Users get the same offline zero-token gemma4 coding plus jcode's swarm, with no Hermes maintenance tax on this lane.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)" --assignee RaggioS 2>&1 | tail -3

aiTitle: jcode-fork-adoption-plan

input.command:
cd "/Users/raimondospatola/Documents/AI Projects/jcode" && gh pr create --repo RaggioS/jcode --base master --head feat/local-gemma4-macos --title "macOS image paste fix (locale-independent) + fork config" --body "## What
Fork customization branch for running jcode as the local-gemma4 coding harness on macOS.

## Changes
- **fix(tui): macOS image paste on non-English locales.** Two clipboard fixes:
  - \`clipboard_image()\`: the embedded AppleScript-ObjC reader failed to parse on non-English system locales (Italian raised \`-2741 'found plural class name'\`), so image paste silently returned nothing. Replaced with **JXA** (\`osascript -l JavaScript\`), whose syntax is locale-independent.
  - \`handle_paste()\`: when a bracketed paste delivers empty text (how macOS / the VS Code integrated terminal report Cmd+V for an image-only clipboard), read the clipboard image and attach it. Safe only for empty text — non-empty pastes keep the upstream Wayland multi-MIME guard.
- **docs: PATCHES.md** tracking fork divergence and the rebase-from-upstream workflow.

## Impact
Cmd+V (and Ctrl+V) attach screenshots in the VS Code terminal on macOS-Italian, which stock upstream drops. The clipboard fix is a clean upstream-PR candidate.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)" --assignee RaggioS 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text: jcode fork **PR #1** aperta. Workstream D (memoria) mentre il build finisce. Aggiorno project_jcode_eval con l'esito adozione + repo/PR:

content.content:
1	---
2	name: pocket-llm
3	description: Local offline AI coding agent in VS Code — Hermes Agent + Ollama (Gemma 4 12B). Pivoted from the old Qwen/llama-cpp multimodal chat.
4	metadata:
5	  type: project
6	---
7	# CURRENT STATE (2026-06-17) — supersedes the legacy notes below
8	
9	pocket-llm is now a **local coding agent**, not the old multimodal chat. Stack:
10	- **Backend**: Ollama (`brew`, `:11434`), model **gemma4:12b** (~7.6GB). Old Qwen3-VL/llama-server
11	  stack abandoned. Custom `gemma4-32k` model deleted.
12	- **Agent**: **Hermes Agent** (NousResearch, MIT) installed at `~/.hermes` via official
13	  `curl … nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash --skip-browser --skip-setup`. CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes`.
14	  Replaced **Aider** (uv tool `aider-chat`, now UNINSTALLED + all configs removed).
15	- **UI = launcher, NOT a webview**: `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/` status-bar button (`localAi.launch`)
16	  starts/pre-warms Ollama, then opens the native `hermes` TUI in a terminal docked as a **parallel
17	  editor column** — `createTerminal({ location: { viewColumn: vscode.ViewColumn.Beside } })`, beside the
18	  editor, NOT the bottom panel, NOT an external window (osascript was tried and reverted). The old custom
19	  streaming webview (`media/webview.js`) is gone. Tools/skills trimmed for small prefill → warm ~8s/turn
20	  (see `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`; all re-activatable).
21	- **Deploy gotcha**: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` NESTS when the dest
22	  dir already exists (makes `.../local-ai-launcher/vscode-extension/`) → VS Code keeps loading the stale
23	  top-level `extension.js`. Deploy by copying CONTENTS: `cp -R vscode-extension/. ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`
24	  (trailing `/.`), then Reload Window.
25	- **Hermes config for local Ollama** (`~/.hermes/`): `config.yaml` model.provider=ollama,
26	  base_url=`http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`, model.default=gemma4:12b, streaming on, reasoning_effort low.
27	  `.env` needs `OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama` + `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`. Our copies
28	  committed in `pocket-llm/hermes/` (config.snippet.yaml, env.sample, SOUL.md).
29	- **CRITICAL perf fix**: agent "stops responding" was `num_ctx 32768` on 16GB (12B+KV swaps).
30	  The extension now launches Ollama with **RAM-aware `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH`** (≥32GB→32768,
31	  ≥16GB→16384, else 8192) + **`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`** (no cold-load each turn). Direct Ollama is
32	  fast (tiny prompt); any agent harness sends a big prefill → that was the slowness, NOT the tool.
33	  Hermes warm ≈ 8s/simple query on 17GB; cold ≈ 28-89s (one-time model load).
34	- **Perf knobs added (2026-06-18)**: launcher also sets **`OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1` + `OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE=q8_0`**
35	  → ~half KV-cache RAM, less swap as the 16k ctx fills (applies on next launcher-started Ollama; stop+relaunch to activate).
36	  Hermes config: `display.interface: tui`, `model_catalog.enabled:false` (offline), `security.tirith_enabled:false`
37	  (binary not installed → inert), `stt.enabled:false` (external STT used), `file_read_max_chars:20000`,
38	  `tool_output.max_bytes:16000` (keep 16k ctx lean → fewer compressions long-session). LSP KEPT on (diagnostics useful).
39	  Base prefill (system prompt ~11KB cached + tool schemas) is at the floor; the 12B is the real speed ceiling.
40	- **Prefill gotcha**: Hermes loads ONE cwd context file by priority HERMES.md>AGENTS.md>CLAUDE.md ([[hermes-agent-docs]]).
41	  Launching Hermes from a project subdir that has a `CLAUDE.md` (e.g. pocket-llm's symlink, 8KB) loads it → fat prefill.
42	  The launcher uses cwd=workspace ROOT, where `HERMES.md` (tuned, ~1.2KB) wins — so the normal button flow is fine.
43	- **Runtimes live in system dirs** (Hermes `~/.hermes`, Ollama models `~/.ollama`), like uv/ollama;
44	  only code+config+README are under `$WS/pocket-llm`.
45	- **Model = gemma4:12b — qwen3-vl:8b evaluated & REJECTED (2026-06-23), don't redo**: deep-researched a
46	  multimodal replacement. (a) Default `qwen3-vl:8b` is the THINKING variant (`RENDERER qwen3-vl-thinking`),
47	  reasoning NOT disable-able via Ollama (`think:false` / `/no_think` ignored) → 20-231s per "Ciao", unusable
48	  interactive on 16GB. (b) `qwen3-vl:8b-instruct` is non-thinking, fast (warm ~5s, "Ciao" 4.6s), multimodal
49	  (vision verified, "Red"), 6.1GB — WORKS, but **gemma4-12B beats it on every directly-comparable benchmark**:
50	  MMLU-Pro 77.2 vs 71.6, GPQA Diamond 78.8 vs 61.9, **LiveCodeBench v6 72.0 vs 39.3**; gemma4 also newer
51	  (Jun 2026, official Google model card) + has audio. Both non-thinking (gemma4 has no CoT either). Conclusion:
52	  **stay on gemma4:12b**. The earlier "qwen3-vl newer+stronger" premise was vs gemma-**3** (60.6/40.9) by mistake;
53	  vs the installed gemma-**4** it's a downgrade. Hermes model-switch mechanics → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
54	- **gemma4 thinking toggle — SOLVED 2026-06-23** (was thought unsuppressible): Ollama ≥0.30.x `/v1` honors
55	  `reasoning_effort` (`none`=off, `low`/`medium`/`high`=on) though it ignores `think`. Hermes' custom-provider
56	  plugin only sent `think:false` → ineffective; patched it to also emit `reasoning_effort`. Now `/reasoning none`
57	  = OFF, `/reasoning low|medium|high` = ON at level. SOURCE patch → re-apply after `hermes update`. Full detail
58	  + re-apply steps → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
59	- **Speed is decode-bound, NOT prefill-bound (measured 2026-06-24)**: warm gemma4:12b on M5 16GB →
60	  prompt-eval **~7300 tok/s** (even 6k-token system prompt = <1s), generation **7.6 tok/s** (sequential).
61	  So latency ≈ output-tokens × 0.13s; a ~170-tok answer = ~22s. **Prefill trimming / prompt-cache preheat give
62	  ~0 gain** — prefill already near-free; Ollama already reuses the static prefix. The ONE preheat that matters is
63	  keeping weights in RAM (`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`, set by the launcher) → kills the 20-30s cold-load. Real levers
64	  left: (1) fewer output tokens — DONE via stronger brevity in SOUL (≈20-25s typical, [[hermes-agent-docs]]);
65	  (2) a faster model. Thinking ON scales time with task (more reasoning tokens = more seconds), by design.
66	- **Fast lane (7-8B) — DEFERRED by user 2026-06-24, keep gemma4:12b default**: idea = add a small model as a
67	  SECOND lane switchable via `/model` for quick coding, gemma4 stays default. Researched the quality gap: it's
68	  specialization vs generality, not simply "smaller=worse". A GENERIC 7-8B is clearly below a 12B on reasoning/
69	  broad knowledge/long-context/multimodal (user's instinct right). BUT a SPECIALIZED coder-7B (e.g.
70	  `qwen2.5-coder:7b`) closes/reverses the gap ON CODE — reports: matches 20B+ models, beats DS-Coder-33B,
71	  HumanEval-Instruct ~85% (Qwen2.5-Coder Technical Report, arxiv 2409.12186). Trade-off: a coder-7B loses gemma4's
72	  vision + general reasoning. So a fast lane is only worth it with a SPECIALIZED coder, for quick code tasks only.
73	  Not built (current 20-25s deemed fine); revisit only if quick-turn latency annoys.
74	- **Sampling tuned — temperature 0.6 / top_p 0.9 (2026-06-24, PR #14)**: gemma4's Ollama default temp is 1.0
75	  (too high → rambly, less deterministic code). Set a single all-purpose middle ground (0.6) — tighter/correct for
76	  code, still flexible for chat — via the custom-provider plugin as TOP-LEVEL OpenAI params (Ollama /v1 honors
77	  top-level temperature, ignores `options.temperature`; determinism-verified). User declined a lower 0.3 ("deve
78	  servire per tutto") and a separate Modelfile/model. Re-applied by the reapply script after `hermes update`.
79	  Details → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
80	
81	---
82	# pocket-llm (legacy notes — Qwen multimodal chat, superseded)
83	
84	Learning PoC: prove a *small dense* model at *low quant* runs on modest hardware,
85	zero API tokens. Private repo `github.com/RaggioS/pocket-llm`, git-ignored subfolder
86	in `$WS/pocket-llm`, own git remote (not part of claude-config). Branch `main`.
87	
88	Born from the "quantize DeepSeek-V4 to 8GB" misconception — that's physically
89	impossible (see [[distributed-inference-tools]]); the viable target is a small dense
90	model. Sibling/learning-project of [[subscription-sharing]].
91	
92	## Shared-config inheritance pattern (reusable for all future projects)
93	
94	Subfolder gets shared rules/hooks/skills via **symlinks**, keeps its own code isolated:
95	- `pocket-llm/.claude -> ../.claude` (symlink) · `pocket-llm/CLAUDE.md -> ../CLAUDE.md` (symlink)
96	- project `.gitignore` lists `.claude` + `CLAUDE.md` → symlinks NOT committed to the
97	  project repo (shared config stays owned by claude-config, no duplication/drift).
98	- parent claude-config `.gitignore` (ignore-all + whitelist) already excludes the
99	  whole project folder → `git check-ignore pocket-llm` ✓; project has own remote.
100	- This is the same precedent as subscription-sharing. Replicate for any new repo.
101	
102	## Stack & layout
103	
104	- Python `>=3.9`, `uv` project (hatchling build). Deps: `llama-cpp-python>=0.3`,
105	  `huggingface-hub>=0.24`. Entry point `pocket-llm = pocket_llm.cli:main`.
106	- **Invoke with `uv run python -m pocket_llm`** (NOT the `pocket-llm` console script).
107	  Root cause: hatchling editable-install `.pth` (absolute path) is silently NOT honored by
108	  CPython 3.14's `site.py` when the project path contains a space (`AI Projects`) → console
109	  script raises `ModuleNotFoundError`. `uv run python -c` works (uv injects path) but the
110	  installed bin doesn't. `python -m` from repo root puts CWD on `sys.path[0]` → flat-layout
111	  package imports directly, no editable/.pth needed. `pocket_llm/__main__.py` enables it.
112	  Layout is flat (`pocket_llm/` at root, NOT `src/`).
113	- `pocket_llm/engine.py`: `Engine` wraps `llama_cpp.Llama`; `stream()` yields
114	  chat chunks, `generate()` returns `GenMetrics` (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens≈chunks,
115	  ttft_s, total_s, peak_rss_mb via `ru_maxrss`; `tokens_per_s` property). `n_gpu_layers=-1`
116	  = all layers on Metal.
117	- `pocket_llm/cli.py`: argparse — one-shot `pocket-llm "prompt"` or REPL; footer
118	  `[ N tok | X tok/s | TTFT ms | peak RAM MB ]` to stderr.
119	- `scripts/download_model.py`: `hf_hub_download` → `./models/`. Default
120	  `unsloth/Qwen3.5-4B-GGUF :: Qwen3.5-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf` (~2.6GB). Default `--n-ctx` 16384
121	  (Qwen3.5 = 262K native / 1M via YaRN, but KV cache ~140KB/tok caps usable ctx ~16-32K on 8GB).
122	- `models/` + `*.gguf` gitignored (never commit weights).
123	
124	## Status
125	
126	- **Verified end-to-end** on the user's M-series Mac: `uv sync` built `llama-cpp-python==0.3.29`
127	  (Metal). Default now **Qwen3.5-4B Q4_K_M** (~2.6GB, no 404); old Qwen2.5-3B gguf deleted.
128	  Real numbers via `python -m pocket_llm` @ n_ctx 16384: **Qwen3.5-4B ~35.8 tok/s, peak RAM
129	  ~3.2 GB, TTFT ~270 ms** (earlier Qwen2.5-3B was ~57–58 tok/s / ~2.3GB). Well under 8GB.
130	  100% free (Apache-2.0 weights, MIT llama.cpp, local/offline, zero API tokens).
131	- `completion_tokens` counts stream chunks (approx tokens).
132	
133	## CCR integration (no new CLI)
134	
135	`pocket_llm/serve.py` → `uv run python -m pocket_llm.serve` exposes the model over an
136	**OpenAI-compatible** API (`127.0.0.1:8080/v1`, model id `qwen3.5-4b-local`), wrapping
137	`llama-cpp-python[server]`. Registered as the `local` provider in CCR (see [[ccr-openrouter]]). CCR routes
138	**per-request** on the model field → a local session (`qwen` = `command ccr code
139	--model "local,qwen3.5-4b-local"`) runs in PARALLEL with cloud `ccr code`, no global
140	switch / no 2nd instance. Files isolated per session via `work` (git worktree).
141	Verified: `/v1/chat/completions` returns a completion. NOT added to the `fallback-models`
142	transformer (would overwrite the model id). Claude Code (official) can't use local models
143	directly — CCR is the only integration point.
144	
145	**End-to-end CCR routing VERIFIED** (2026-06-15): request to CCR's Anthropic endpoint
146	(`127.0.0.1:3456/v1/messages`) with `Router.default='local,qwen3.5-4b-local'` → routed
147	through local provider → llama server → reply `pong`, response `model=qwen3.5-4b-local`.
148	Default restored to OpenRouter afterwards (local is opt-in via `ccr-local`).
149	
150	## Serve stability — DO NOT re-enable flash_attn/cache/big batch
151	
152	Qwen3.5-4B is a **hybrid SSM/attention** model. `--flash_attn true` triggers a Metal
153	kernel-compilation storm → server crashes (empty reply, port held by orphan
154	`llama_cpp.server` child). `--n_batch 2048 + --cache true` together also crash it.
155	**Verified-stable serve config**: `n_ctx 32768`, `n_batch 1024`, no flash_attn, no cache
156	(committed in `serve.py`, exposes `--n-batch`; 2048 only crashes WITH flash+cache). Bench:
157	floor latency 0.9s, 7019-token prefill ~40s (~176 tok/s), server stays UP. Prefill scales
158	linearly → Claude-Code's ~26k-token/turn context ≈ ~150s/turn (4B prefill is physics, not a
159	bug). The earlier "non risponde quasi mai" was the flash/cache crash + content-array 500
160	([[ccr-openrouter]] `local-content-fix`), now both fixed. Kill BOTH on stop:
161	`pkill -f pocket_llm.serve; pkill -f llama_cpp.server`.
162	
163	**Two UX gotchas (not server bugs):** (1) llama-cpp-python serves ONE request at a time —
164	a big in-flight turn blocks new ones; Claude Code's UI interrupt does NOT cancel server-side
165	inference (HTTP disconnect ignored), so a stuck turn keeps the slot busy → restart server to
166	clear it. (2) Biggest user-side speed lever is `/effort low` in the session (medium makes a
167	4B emit long reasoning → minutes). RAM is NOT a lever: model is 3.2GB, fully on Metal,
168	compute-bound not memory-bound — adding RAM does nothing. Local = offline/simple; fast
169	interactive work = cloud.
170	
171	## DECISION (2026-06-15): local = offline/experiment only; interactive = cloud
172	
173	Settled after the user hit ~2min/turn on a plain "ciao" in Claude Code. Proof: the RAW
174	model answers "ciao" in **0.36s**; the same "ciao" via Claude Code/CCR carries ~26k tokens
175	of system+tools harness → ~150s prefill, and the hybrid SSM can't cache it. No param fixes
176	this — it's structural. **User chose: keep local installed for offline/test, do all
177	interactive work on cloud (Claude Code Opus + CCR→OpenRouter, already the default).** Do NOT
178	re-pitch local-in-Claude-Code as "fast"; it is reliable, not fast. Local server is normally
179	STOPPED (relaunch via the VS Code extension only when offline). A stuck llama_cpp.server
180	ignores SIGTERM mid-Metal-compute → needs `kill -9`.
181	
182	## VS Code extension = native chat webview (v0.0.6, model-agnostic)
183	
184	**Lives in `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/`** (RaggioS/pocket-llm@main — MOVED here from
185	claude-config so the whole product is one repo; deploy: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` + Reload Window). Now v0.0.16. Big webview script is `vscode-extension/media/webview.js` (real file via asWebviewUri — avoids template-literal escaping). Features added: streaming SSE, stop-gen, multi-session panels (each launch = new panel/session), in-panel history overlay (open/delete), markdown + code blocks (copy/open-editor/save-file), personas + "about me" profile + temp/max_tokens (globalState), text/code file context, image input. Live copy `~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`.
186	Repointed from `ccr code --model local` (Claude Code harness, ~26k tok/turn, minutes) to a
187	**native webview chat** that POSTs DIRECTLY to `127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions` via
188	extension↔webview message-passing (no CCR, no harness) → ~2s replies, offline, zero token.
189	Button/status-bar `localAi.launch` opens the panel; auto-starts server + polls readiness;
190	stop kills both processes. **Model-agnostic**: UI says "Local AI", the model is only in
191	setting `localAi.modelId` (swap a future better local model with one setting, no rename).
192	Other settings: `serverUrl`, `serveDir`, `maxTokens` (512), `systemPrompt` (distilled house
193	rules — do NOT paste full CLAUDE.md/memories here, it re-bloats context and kills speed).
194	What CANNOT carry to a raw chat: hooks, skills, status-line (Claude Code machinery); only
195	CLAUDE.md/memory *content* can, as a short system prompt. Bump version to force VS Code reload.
196	
197	## MULTIMODAL (2026-06-15): Qwen3-VL-8B via llama-server, text + images
198	
199	Local chat is now **multimodal**. Model swapped from text-only Qwen3.5-4B to **Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct
200	Q4_K_M** (`models/qwen3-vl-8b/`, ~4.7GB + mmproj-F16 ~1.1GB). KEY: `llama-cpp-python` does NOT
201	support Qwen3-VL → serve via llama.cpp's native **`llama-server`** (`brew install llama.cpp`,
202	binary at `/opt/homebrew/bin/llama-server`, also `llama-mtmd-cli`). New wrapper
203	`pocket_llm/serve_vision.py` runs `llama-server -m <model> --mmproj <proj> -ngl 99 -c 8192 --jinja`,
204	OpenAI-compatible on :8080, model id `qwen3-vl-8b-local`. Accepts `image_url` content parts
205	(data URLs). Verified e2e: text ~1s/27 tok/s, image (red square → "Rosso") ~0.6s, server UP.
206	Extension (v0.0.9) starts `serve_vision`, panel has image input (attach 📎 / paste / drag-drop,
207	thumbnails, bubbles render images, history persists multimodal msgs). stopServer kills
208	`serve_vision`+`llama-server`. Old text path (`serve.py`, Qwen3.5-4B gguf) still on disk as
209	fallback, now superseded.
210	
211	## Bench + UX polish (2026-06-15, panel v0.0.11)
212	
213	Bench Qwen3-VL-8B (M-series, 17GB): RAM RSS **4.1GB**; gen **~25 tok/s** costante (corto=lungo);
214	TTFT testo corto 54–464ms (caldo ~50ms); prefill grande ~3700 tok = **TTFT 12.7s** (collo
215	bottiglia); immagine 64px 0.6s, immagine reale 1024×768 **4.5s**. **1 solo slot** → richieste
216	concorrenti serializzano. Panel ora: **streaming SSE** (postChatStream, token live), **bottone
217	Stop** (Invia→Stop rosso durante gen, aborta req, tiene parziale), **indicatore prefill pulsante**
218	(bolla "…" fade, copre l'attesa muta immagini grandi). maxTokens setting default 512.
219	
220	## Status: COMPLETE & shipped
221	
222	Repo `RaggioS/pocket-llm` (private). Full reproducible README (Requirements,
223	install-on-another-device, GPU backends, CCR integration + toggle, troubleshooting, tests),
224	LICENSE (MIT), smoke tests green (`uv run --extra dev pytest`). Engine + CLI + REPL + serve,
225	default Qwen3.5-4B Q4. CCR local provider live + e2e-verified. Nothing pending to ship.
226	
227	## Roadmap (optional future)
228	
229	Bench harness (sweep Q2/Q4/Q5/Q8 → tok/s vs RAM vs quality), "does it fit?" pre-flight
230	(file size + free RAM before load), CPU-only mode bench for low-end/Windows machines.
231	- **Richer Hermes prefill — DONE & validated 2026-06-23**: added a "Per-project quick facts" section to
232	  `HERMES.md` (run commands + key gotchas per project) so Hermes answers grounded questions without runtime
233	  discovery tool-calls. Measured: HERMES.md 1592→2477 B, +232 prompt tokens, **warm prefill +8ms** (172→181ms;
234	  prompt-cache absorbs it), cold +845ms once per session. Grounding verified: "how to run pocket-llm" →
235	  `uv run python -m pocket_llm`, no tools, top behavioral rules NOT diluted. Verdict: worth it, kept. Sweet
236	  spot reached — pushing further adds cold cost + dilution risk for little gain. Method for future tweaks:
237	  measure warm `prompt_eval_duration` x2 (2nd = cached) before/after; do NOT dump CLAUDE.md/memories (re-bloats).
238	
239	**Triggers:** pocket-llm, quantized local LLM, Qwen 3B Q4, llama-cpp-python, 8GB LLM, GGUF, tok/s bench, local inference PoC
240	**Why:** new ongoing project outliving the task; documents the symlink shared-config pattern reused by future repos.
241	**How to apply:** next session on this repo, resume from roadmap; for a NEW separate project replicate the symlink+gitignore pattern above. Use `( cd pocket-llm && ... )` subshell per [[bash-cwd-hooks]].
242

---
name: pocket-llm
description: Local offline AI coding agent in VS Code — Hermes Agent + Ollama (Gemma 4 12B). Pivoted from the old Qwen/llama-cpp multimodal chat.
metadata:
  type: project
---
# CURRENT STATE (2026-06-17) — supersedes the legacy notes below

pocket-llm is now a **local coding agent**, not the old multimodal chat. Stack:
- **Backend**: Ollama (`brew`, `:11434`), model **gemma4:12b** (~7.6GB). Old Qwen3-VL/llama-server
  stack abandoned. Custom `gemma4-32k` model deleted.
- **Agent**: **Hermes Agent** (NousResearch, MIT) installed at `~/.hermes` via official
  `curl … nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash --skip-browser --skip-setup`. CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes`.
  Replaced **Aider** (uv tool `aider-chat`, now UNINSTALLED + all configs removed).
- **UI = launcher, NOT a webview**: `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/` status-bar button (`localAi.launch`)
  starts/pre-warms Ollama, then opens the native `hermes` TUI in a terminal docked as a **parallel
  editor column** — `createTerminal({ location: { viewColumn: vscode.ViewColumn.Beside } })`, beside the
  editor, NOT the bottom panel, NOT an external window (osascript was tried and reverted). The old custom
  streaming webview (`media/webview.js`) is gone. Tools/skills trimmed for small prefill → warm ~8s/turn
  (see `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`; all re-activatable).
- **Deploy gotcha**: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` NESTS when the dest
  dir already exists (makes `.../local-ai-launcher/vscode-extension/`) → VS Code keeps loading the stale
  top-level `extension.js`. Deploy by copying CONTENTS: `cp -R vscode-extension/. ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`
  (trailing `/.`), then Reload Window.
- **Hermes config for local Ollama** (`~/.hermes/`): `config.yaml` model.provider=ollama,
  base_url=`http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`, model.default=gemma4:12b, streaming on, reasoning_effort low.
  `.env` needs `OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama` + `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`. Our copies
  committed in `pocket-llm/hermes/` (config.snippet.yaml, env.sample, SOUL.md).
- **CRITICAL perf fix**: agent "stops responding" was `num_ctx 32768` on 16GB (12B+KV swaps).
  The extension now launches Ollama with **RAM-aware `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH`** (≥32GB→32768,
  ≥16GB→16384, else 8192) + **`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`** (no cold-load each turn). Direct Ollama is
  fast (tiny prompt); any agent harness sends a big prefill → that was the slowness, NOT the tool.
  Hermes warm ≈ 8s/simple query on 17GB; cold ≈ 28-89s (one-time model load).
- **Perf knobs added (2026-06-18)**: launcher also sets **`OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1` + `OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE=q8_0`**
  → ~half KV-cache RAM, less swap as the 16k ctx fills (applies on next launcher-started Ollama; stop+relaunch to activate).
  Hermes config: `display.interface: tui`, `model_catalog.enabled:false` (offline), `security.tirith_enabled:false`
  (binary not installed → inert), `stt.enabled:false` (external STT used), `file_read_max_chars:20000`,
  `tool_output.max_bytes:16000` (keep 16k ctx lean → fewer compressions long-session). LSP KEPT on (diagnostics useful).
  Base prefill (system prompt ~11KB cached + tool schemas) is at the floor; the 12B is the real speed ceiling.
- **Prefill gotcha**: Hermes loads ONE cwd context file by priority HERMES.md>AGENTS.md>CLAUDE.md ([[hermes-agent-docs]]).
  Launching Hermes from a project subdir that has a `CLAUDE.md` (e.g. pocket-llm's symlink, 8KB) loads it → fat prefill.
  The launcher uses cwd=workspace ROOT, where `HERMES.md` (tuned, ~1.2KB) wins — so the normal button flow is fine.
- **Runtimes live in system dirs** (Hermes `~/.hermes`, Ollama models `~/.ollama`), like uv/ollama;
  only code+config+README are under `$WS/pocket-llm`.
- **Model = gemma4:12b — qwen3-vl:8b evaluated & REJECTED (2026-06-23), don't redo**: deep-researched a
  multimodal replacement. (a) Default `qwen3-vl:8b` is the THINKING variant (`RENDERER qwen3-vl-thinking`),
  reasoning NOT disable-able via Ollama (`think:false` / `/no_think` ignored) → 20-231s per "Ciao", unusable
  interactive on 16GB. (b) `qwen3-vl:8b-instruct` is non-thinking, fast (warm ~5s, "Ciao" 4.6s), multimodal
  (vision verified, "Red"), 6.1GB — WORKS, but **gemma4-12B beats it on every directly-comparable benchmark**:
  MMLU-Pro 77.2 vs 71.6, GPQA Diamond 78.8 vs 61.9, **LiveCodeBench v6 72.0 vs 39.3**; gemma4 also newer
  (Jun 2026, official Google model card) + has audio. Both non-thinking (gemma4 has no CoT either). Conclusion:
  **stay on gemma4:12b**. The earlier "qwen3-vl newer+stronger" premise was vs gemma-**3** (60.6/40.9) by mistake;
  vs the installed gemma-**4** it's a downgrade. Hermes model-switch mechanics → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
- **gemma4 thinking toggle — SOLVED 2026-06-23** (was thought unsuppressible): Ollama ≥0.30.x `/v1` honors
  `reasoning_effort` (`none`=off, `low`/`medium`/`high`=on) though it ignores `think`. Hermes' custom-provider
  plugin only sent `think:false` → ineffective; patched it to also emit `reasoning_effort`. Now `/reasoning none`
  = OFF, `/reasoning low|medium|high` = ON at level. SOURCE patch → re-apply after `hermes update`. Full detail
  + re-apply steps → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
- **Speed is decode-bound, NOT prefill-bound (measured 2026-06-24)**: warm gemma4:12b on M5 16GB →
  prompt-eval **~7300 tok/s** (even 6k-token system prompt = <1s), generation **7.6 tok/s** (sequential).
  So latency ≈ output-tokens × 0.13s; a ~170-tok answer = ~22s. **Prefill trimming / prompt-cache preheat give
  ~0 gain** — prefill already near-free; Ollama already reuses the static prefix. The ONE preheat that matters is
  keeping weights in RAM (`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`, set by the launcher) → kills the 20-30s cold-load. Real levers
  left: (1) fewer output tokens — DONE via stronger brevity in SOUL (≈20-25s typical, [[hermes-agent-docs]]);
  (2) a faster model. Thinking ON scales time with task (more reasoning tokens = more seconds), by design.
- **Fast lane (7-8B) — DEFERRED by user 2026-06-24, keep gemma4:12b default**: idea = add a small model as a
  SECOND lane switchable via `/model` for quick coding, gemma4 stays default. Researched the quality gap: it's
  specialization vs generality, not simply "smaller=worse". A GENERIC 7-8B is clearly below a 12B on reasoning/
  broad knowledge/long-context/multimodal (user's instinct right). BUT a SPECIALIZED coder-7B (e.g.
  `qwen2.5-coder:7b`) closes/reverses the gap ON CODE — reports: matches 20B+ models, beats DS-Coder-33B,
  HumanEval-Instruct ~85% (Qwen2.5-Coder Technical Report, arxiv 2409.12186). Trade-off: a coder-7B loses gemma4's
  vision + general reasoning. So a fast lane is only worth it with a SPECIALIZED coder, for quick code tasks only.
  Not built (current 20-25s deemed fine); revisit only if quick-turn latency annoys.
- **Sampling tuned — temperature 0.6 / top_p 0.9 (2026-06-24, PR #14)**: gemma4's Ollama default temp is 1.0
  (too high → rambly, less deterministic code). Set a single all-purpose middle ground (0.6) — tighter/correct for
  code, still flexible for chat — via the custom-provider plugin as TOP-LEVEL OpenAI params (Ollama /v1 honors
  top-level temperature, ignores `options.temperature`; determinism-verified). User declined a lower 0.3 ("deve
  servire per tutto") and a separate Modelfile/model. Re-applied by the reapply script after `hermes update`.
  Details → [[hermes-agent-docs]].

# pocket-llm (legacy notes — Qwen multimodal chat, superseded)

Learning PoC: prove a *small dense* model at *low quant* runs on modest hardware,
zero API tokens. Private repo `github.com/RaggioS/pocket-llm`, git-ignored subfolder
in `$WS/pocket-llm`, own git remote (not part of claude-config). Branch `main`.

Born from the "quantize DeepSeek-V4 to 8GB" misconception — that's physically
impossible (see [[distributed-inference-tools]]); the viable target is a small dense
model. Sibling/learning-project of [[subscription-sharing]].

## Shared-config inheritance pattern (reusable for all future projects)

Subfolder gets shared rules/hooks/skills via **symlinks**, keeps its own code isolated:
- `pocket-llm/.claude -> ../.claude` (symlink) · `pocket-llm/CLAUDE.md -> ../CLAUDE.md` (symlink)
- project `.gitignore` lists `.claude` + `CLAUDE.md` → symlinks NOT committed to the
  project repo (shared config stays owned by claude-config, no duplication/drift).
- parent claude-config `.gitignore` (ignore-all + whitelist) already excludes the
  whole project folder → `git check-ignore pocket-llm` ✓; project has own remote.
- This is the same precedent as subscription-sharing. Replicate for any new repo.

## Stack & layout

- Python `>=3.9`, `uv` project (hatchling build). Deps: `llama-cpp-python>=0.3`,
  `huggingface-hub>=0.24`. Entry point `pocket-llm = pocket_llm.cli:main`.
- **Invoke with `uv run python -m pocket_llm`** (NOT the `pocket-llm` console script).
  Root cause: hatchling editable-install `.pth` (absolute path) is silently NOT honored by
  CPython 3.14's `site.py` when the project path contains a space (`AI Projects`) → console
  script raises `ModuleNotFoundError`. `uv run python -c` works (uv injects path) but the
  installed bin doesn't. `python -m` from repo root puts CWD on `sys.path[0]` → flat-layout
  package imports directly, no editable/.pth needed. `pocket_llm/__main__.py` enables it.
  Layout is flat (`pocket_llm/` at root, NOT `src/`).
- `pocket_llm/engine.py`: `Engine` wraps `llama_cpp.Llama`; `stream()` yields
  chat chunks, `generate()` returns `GenMetrics` (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens≈chunks,
  ttft_s, total_s, peak_rss_mb via `ru_maxrss`; `tokens_per_s` property). `n_gpu_layers=-1`
  = all layers on Metal.
- `pocket_llm/cli.py`: argparse — one-shot `pocket-llm "prompt"` or REPL; footer
  `[ N tok | X tok/s | TTFT ms | peak RAM MB ]` to stderr.
- `scripts/download_model.py`: `hf_hub_download` → `./models/`. Default
  `unsloth/Qwen3.5-4B-GGUF :: Qwen3.5-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf` (~2.6GB). Default `--n-ctx` 16384
  (Qwen3.5 = 262K native / 1M via YaRN, but KV cache ~140KB/tok caps usable ctx ~16-32K on 8GB).
- `models/` + `*.gguf` gitignored (never commit weights).

## Status

- **Verified end-to-end** on the user's M-series Mac: `uv sync` built `llama-cpp-python==0.3.29`
  (Metal). Default now **Qwen3.5-4B Q4_K_M** (~2.6GB, no 404); old Qwen2.5-3B gguf deleted.
  Real numbers via `python -m pocket_llm` @ n_ctx 16384: **Qwen3.5-4B ~35.8 tok/s, peak RAM
  ~3.2 GB, TTFT ~270 ms** (earlier Qwen2.5-3B was ~57–58 tok/s / ~2.3GB). Well under 8GB.
  100% free (Apache-2.0 weights, MIT llama.cpp, local/offline, zero API tokens).
- `completion_tokens` counts stream chunks (approx tokens).

## CCR integration (no new CLI)

`pocket_llm/serve.py` → `uv run python -m pocket_llm.serve` exposes the model over an
**OpenAI-compatible** API (`127.0.0.1:8080/v1`, model id `qwen3.5-4b-local`), wrapping
`llama-cpp-python[server]`. Registered as the `local` provider in CCR (see [[ccr-openrouter]]). CCR routes
**per-request** on the model field → a local session (`qwen` = `command ccr code
--model "local,qwen3.5-4b-local"`) runs in PARALLEL with cloud `ccr code`, no global
switch / no 2nd instance. Files isolated per session via `work` (git worktree).
Verified: `/v1/chat/completions` returns a completion. NOT added to the `fallback-models`
transformer (would overwrite the model id). Claude Code (official) can't use local models
directly — CCR is the only integration point.

**End-to-end CCR routing VERIFIED** (2026-06-15): request to CCR's Anthropic endpoint
(`127.0.0.1:3456/v1/messages`) with `Router.default='local,qwen3.5-4b-local'` → routed
through local provider → llama server → reply `pong`, response `model=qwen3.5-4b-local`.
Default restored to OpenRouter afterwards (local is opt-in via `ccr-local`).

## Serve stability — DO NOT re-enable flash_attn/cache/big batch

Qwen3.5-4B is a **hybrid SSM/attention** model. `--flash_attn true` triggers a Metal
kernel-compilation storm → server crashes (empty reply, port held by orphan
`llama_cpp.server` child). `--n_batch 2048 + --cache true` together also crash it.
**Verified-stable serve config**: `n_ctx 32768`, `n_batch 1024`, no flash_attn, no cache
(committed in `serve.py`, exposes `--n-batch`; 2048 only crashes WITH flash+cache). Bench:
floor latency 0.9s, 7019-token prefill ~40s (~176 tok/s), server stays UP. Prefill scales
linearly → Claude-Code's ~26k-token/turn context ≈ ~150s/turn (4B prefill is physics, not a
bug). The earlier "non risponde quasi mai" was the flash/cache crash + content-array 500
([[ccr-openrouter]] `local-content-fix`), now both fixed. Kill BOTH on stop:
`pkill -f pocket_llm.serve; pkill -f llama_cpp.server`.

**Two UX gotchas (not server bugs):** (1) llama-cpp-python serves ONE request at a time —
a big in-flight turn blocks new ones; Claude Code's UI interrupt does NOT cancel server-side
inference (HTTP disconnect ignored), so a stuck turn keeps the slot busy → restart server to
clear it. (2) Biggest user-side speed lever is `/effort low` in the session (medium makes a
4B emit long reasoning → minutes). RAM is NOT a lever: model is 3.2GB, fully on Metal,
compute-bound not memory-bound — adding RAM does nothing. Local = offline/simple; fast
interactive work = cloud.

## DECISION (2026-06-15): local = offline/experiment only; interactive = cloud

Settled after the user hit ~2min/turn on a plain "ciao" in Claude Code. Proof: the RAW
model answers "ciao" in **0.36s**; the same "ciao" via Claude Code/CCR carries ~26k tokens
of system+tools harness → ~150s prefill, and the hybrid SSM can't cache it. No param fixes
this — it's structural. **User chose: keep local installed for offline/test, do all
interactive work on cloud (Claude Code Opus + CCR→OpenRouter, already the default).** Do NOT
re-pitch local-in-Claude-Code as "fast"; it is reliable, not fast. Local server is normally
STOPPED (relaunch via the VS Code extension only when offline). A stuck llama_cpp.server
ignores SIGTERM mid-Metal-compute → needs `kill -9`.

## VS Code extension = native chat webview (v0.0.6, model-agnostic)

**Lives in `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/`** (RaggioS/pocket-llm@main — MOVED here from
claude-config so the whole product is one repo; deploy: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` + Reload Window). Now v0.0.16. Big webview script is `vscode-extension/media/webview.js` (real file via asWebviewUri — avoids template-literal escaping). Features added: streaming SSE, stop-gen, multi-session panels (each launch = new panel/session), in-panel history overlay (open/delete), markdown + code blocks (copy/open-editor/save-file), personas + "about me" profile + temp/max_tokens (globalState), text/code file context, image input. Live copy `~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`.
Repointed from `ccr code --model local` (Claude Code harness, ~26k tok/turn, minutes) to a
**native webview chat** that POSTs DIRECTLY to `127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions` via
extension↔webview message-passing (no CCR, no harness) → ~2s replies, offline, zero token.
Button/status-bar `localAi.launch` opens the panel; auto-starts server + polls readiness;
stop kills both processes. **Model-agnostic**: UI says "Local AI", the model is only in
setting `localAi.modelId` (swap a future better local model with one setting, no rename).
Other settings: `serverUrl`, `serveDir`, `maxTokens` (512), `systemPrompt` (distilled house
rules — do NOT paste full CLAUDE.md/memories here, it re-bloats context and kills speed).
What CANNOT carry to a raw chat: hooks, skills, status-line (Claude Code machinery); only
CLAUDE.md/memory *content* can, as a short system prompt. Bump version to force VS Code reload.

## MULTIMODAL (2026-06-15): Qwen3-VL-8B via llama-server, text + images

Local chat is now **multimodal**. Model swapped from text-only Qwen3.5-4B to **Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct
Q4_K_M** (`models/qwen3-vl-8b/`, ~4.7GB + mmproj-F16 ~1.1GB). KEY: `llama-cpp-python` does NOT
support Qwen3-VL → serve via llama.cpp's native **`llama-server`** (`brew install llama.cpp`,
binary at `/opt/homebrew/bin/llama-server`, also `llama-mtmd-cli`). New wrapper
`pocket_llm/serve_vision.py` runs `llama-server -m <model> --mmproj <proj> -ngl 99 -c 8192 --jinja`,
OpenAI-compatible on :8080, model id `qwen3-vl-8b-local`. Accepts `image_url` content parts
(data URLs). Verified e2e: text ~1s/27 tok/s, image (red square → "Rosso") ~0.6s, server UP.
Extension (v0.0.9) starts `serve_vision`, panel has image input (attach 📎 / paste / drag-drop,
thumbnails, bubbles render images, history persists multimodal msgs). stopServer kills
`serve_vision`+`llama-server`. Old text path (`serve.py`, Qwen3.5-4B gguf) still on disk as
fallback, now superseded.

## Bench + UX polish (2026-06-15, panel v0.0.11)

Bench Qwen3-VL-8B (M-series, 17GB): RAM RSS **4.1GB**; gen **~25 tok/s** costante (corto=lungo);
TTFT testo corto 54–464ms (caldo ~50ms); prefill grande ~3700 tok = **TTFT 12.7s** (collo
bottiglia); immagine 64px 0.6s, immagine reale 1024×768 **4.5s**. **1 solo slot** → richieste
concorrenti serializzano. Panel ora: **streaming SSE** (postChatStream, token live), **bottone
Stop** (Invia→Stop rosso durante gen, aborta req, tiene parziale), **indicatore prefill pulsante**
(bolla "…" fade, copre l'attesa muta immagini grandi). maxTokens setting default 512.

## Status: COMPLETE & shipped

Repo `RaggioS/pocket-llm` (private). Full reproducible README (Requirements,
install-on-another-device, GPU backends, CCR integration + toggle, troubleshooting, tests),
LICENSE (MIT), smoke tests green (`uv run --extra dev pytest`). Engine + CLI + REPL + serve,
default Qwen3.5-4B Q4. CCR local provider live + e2e-verified. Nothing pending to ship.

## Roadmap (optional future)

Bench harness (sweep Q2/Q4/Q5/Q8 → tok/s vs RAM vs quality), "does it fit?" pre-flight
(file size + free RAM before load), CPU-only mode bench for low-end/Windows machines.
- **Richer Hermes prefill — DONE & validated 2026-06-23**: added a "Per-project quick facts" section to
  `HERMES.md` (run commands + key gotchas per project) so Hermes answers grounded questions without runtime
  discovery tool-calls. Measured: HERMES.md 1592→2477 B, +232 prompt tokens, **warm prefill +8ms** (172→181ms;
  prompt-cache absorbs it), cold +845ms once per session. Grounding verified: "how to run pocket-llm" →
  `uv run python -m pocket_llm`, no tools, top behavioral rules NOT diluted. Verdict: worth it, kept. Sweet
  spot reached — pushing further adds cold cost + dilution risk for little gain. Method for future tweaks:
  measure warm `prompt_eval_duration` x2 (2nd = cached) before/after; do NOT dump CLAUDE.md/memories (re-bloats).

**Triggers:** pocket-llm, quantized local LLM, Qwen 3B Q4, llama-cpp-python, 8GB LLM, GGUF, tok/s bench, local inference PoC
**Why:** new ongoing project outliving the task; documents the symlink shared-config pattern reused by future repos.
**How to apply:** next session on this repo, resume from roadmap; for a NEW separate project replicate the symlink+gitignore pattern above. Use `( cd pocket-llm && ... )` subshell per [[bash-cwd-hooks]].

content.text: project_pocket_llm è grande e Hermes-centrico. Aggiungo blocco CURRENT STATE 2026-06-25 in cima (supersede), aggiorno description:

# CURRENT STATE (2026-06-25) — supersedes the 2026-06-17 Hermes notes below

pocket-llm's launcher now drives **jcode** (RaggioS fork, Rust harness), NOT Hermes, for the local gemma4 lane.
Decision after a full eval (see [[jcode-eval]]): jcode ties Hermes on gemma4 speed (model-bound) but adds native
multi-agent/swarm + a lighter footprint; the user adopts jcode for local gemma4 and keeps Hermes ONLY for the
ccr-free-router Gemini lane ([[ccr-free-router-repo]]).
- **Branch/PR**: `feat/jcode-launcher` → `RaggioS/pocket-llm` **PR #16**. Bumped ext 0.1.1→0.2.0, project 0.2.0→0.3.0.
- **`vscode-extension/extension.js`**: opens the jcode TUI (`jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b`) in a
  parallel editor column; status button now "$(rocket) jcode". Ollama lifecycle UNCHANGED (RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH`, `KEEP_ALIVE=30m`, flash-attn + q8_0 KV, model pre-warm). Hermes thinking-patch reapply
  REMOVED. Env sets `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` + `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY={"reasoning_effort":"none"}` (thinking off).
- **`jcode/`** (replaces `hermes/`): `config.toml` (→`~/.jcode/config.toml`: provider ollama-local, gemma4:12b,
  `extra_body.reasoning_effort="none"`, ctx 16384), `AGENTS.md` (→`~/AGENTS.md`: lean Italian persona — jcode loads
  `~/AGENTS.md` + project `AGENTS.md`, `jcode-base/src/prompt.rs`), `env.sample`. Removed `hermes/`,
  `scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh`, `vscode-extension/HERMES.md` (→ new `JCODE.md`).
- **jcode binary**: built/installed from fork [[jcode-eval]] / [[jcode-fork]] (`~/.local/bin/jcode`). Thinking-off,
  telemetry-off, macOS image paste fixed (2 patches). Same Ollama backend, same gemma4:12b, same ~8s warm.
- Everything below this block (Hermes config, thinking-patch, sampling) is HISTORICAL — applies to the Hermes lane
  that moved to ccr-free-router, not to pocket-llm anymore.
