---
name: jcode-fork
description: RaggioS/jcode fork — our customized jcode (Rust coding harness) for the local gemma4 lane. Build/install, config, patches, rebase-from-upstream.
metadata:
  type: reference
---

# jcode (RaggioS fork) — operational reference

Our personal fork of [1jehuang/jcode](https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode) = the **sole local-gemma4 coding
harness** (replaced Hermes for that lane 2026-06-25; eval → [[jcode-eval]]). MIT, Rust. Upstream is a
single-author project; we own the fork so our patches are durable + rebase-able.

## Repos / locations
- Fork: `https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode` (remote `origin`); upstream `https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode` (remote `upstream`).
- Working clone: **`$WS/jcode`** (git-ignored by `$WS` ignore-all, own remote — same pattern as pocket-llm/ccr-free-router).
- Branch with our changes: **`feat/local-gemma4-macos`** (PR #1 on the fork). Rebase this on `upstream/master`.
- Installed binary: `~/.local/bin/jcode` → `~/.jcode/builds/current/jcode` (via `scripts/install_release.sh`).
- App data/config: `~/.jcode/` (config.toml, logs, sessions, skills, mcp.json). Global persona: `~/AGENTS.md`.

## Build / install / update
```bash
cd "$WS/jcode"
PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" cargo build --release --bin jcode   # ~5 min cold, ~10s incremental
scripts/install_release.sh                                          # → ~/.local/bin/jcode
# update from upstream:
git fetch upstream && git rebase upstream/master   # on feat/local-gemma4-macos, then rebuild+install
```
Rust installed via `brew install rust` (1.96). Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in PATH.

## Our patches (vs upstream) — see fork `PATCHES.md`
- **macOS image paste on non-English locales** (`crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/{helpers.rs,input.rs}`):
  - `clipboard_image()`: AppleScript-ObjC → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`). AppleScript dialect errored on
    Italian locale (`-2741 found plural class name`) → silent image-paste failure. JXA is locale-independent.
  - `handle_paste()`: empty bracketed paste (macOS/VS Code Cmd+V with image-only clipboard = 0-char paste) →
    read clipboard image + attach. Cmd+V AND Ctrl+V now attach screenshots. Good upstream-PR candidate.

## Runtime config (machine-local; canonical templates in `pocket-llm/jcode/`)
- **Thinking off** for gemma4: Ollama `/v1` honors top-level `reasoning_effort`. Set in `~/.jcode/config.toml`
  (`[providers.ollama-local] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`) and/or env
  `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` (the launcher sets the env).
- **Telemetry off**: `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (jcode collects anonymous stats by default).
- **Italian persona**: `~/AGENTS.md` (global, loaded into every system prompt) + project `AGENTS.md`.
- **Run local**: `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b` (TUI) — no login/key (Ollama = local endpoint).
- **Swarm**: implemented (`jcode-swarm-core` channels/DM/broadcast + `jcode-terminal-launch`). Caveat: N gemma4
  agents on one Mac serialize on the GPU/Ollama → for real concurrency point swarm workers at OpenRouter free.

## Launcher
pocket-llm VS Code extension (robot button) launches jcode for the gemma4 lane → [[pocket-llm]].

[[jcode-eval]] [[pocket-llm]] [[hermes-agent-docs]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode fork, RaggioS jcode, build jcode, install jcode, jcode config, jcode thinking off, jcode image paste, jcode swarm, rebase jcode upstream, local gemma4 harness
**Why:** jcode is now the daily local-gemma4 coding harness; the operational details (build/install/config/patches) must not be re-derived each session.
**How to apply:** for any local-gemma4 jcode task, work in `$WS/jcode` on `feat/local-gemma4-macos`; rebuild+install after changes; rebase from upstream periodically; config templates live in `pocket-llm/jcode/`.

description: jcode eval → ADOPTED for the local gemma4 lane (2026-06-25). Decision rationale + measured findings. Operational details in [[jcode-fork]].

# OUTCOME (2026-06-25): ADOPTED for the local gemma4 lane

After the full eval below, decision flipped from "keep Hermes" to **adopt jcode** for local gemma4. The
deciding factor: jcode has **native multi-agent/swarm** (verified implemented — `jcode-swarm-core` channels/
DM/broadcast + `jcode-terminal-launch` + TUI widgets; the "Proposed" doc header is stale vs the code), which the
user's trimmed Hermes lacks (delegation disabled for speed). Speed parity (model-bound) was NOT the tiebreaker;
the new capability + user's willingness to own a fork (neutralizes the bus-factor/maintenance objection) was.
- Two earlier "keep Hermes" Haiku agents were right on maturity but under-weighted swarm (one misread the stale doc).
- **Split**: local gemma4 = SOLO jcode; **Hermes stays ONLY for the ccr-free-router Gemini/FreeCode lane**.
- **Done**: fork `RaggioS/jcode` (branch `feat/local-gemma4-macos`, PR #1) with the 2 macOS image patches +
  PATCHES.md; pocket-llm switched to jcode launcher (`RaggioS/pocket-llm` PR #16). Operational ref → [[jcode-fork]].
- Caveat carried forward: gemma4 swarm on one Mac serializes (GPU-bound) → point swarm workers at OpenRouter free.

---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement (history)

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: jcode-eval
description: Evaluating jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) as a lighter replacement for Hermes driving local gemma4. In-flight experiment + Hermes rollback baseline.
metadata:
  type: project
---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model
coding harness, driving `gemma4:12b` on Ollama (or a future local model). Hypothesis: jcode is
much lighter/faster (Rust, ~27 MB RAM embedding-off vs Hermes' Python/Node stack), so response
and eval latency should improve. Evaluate REAL impact before committing.

## What jcode is
- Repo: https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode — MIT, single-author (Jeremy Huang), very active.
- Rust coding-agent harness (Codex/Claude-Code category), NOT an LLM. BYO-model: ~40 OpenAI-compatible
  providers + native Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter. Free path = local Ollama / LM Studio / OpenRouter `:free`.
- Ollama profile: api_base `http://localhost:11434/v1`, auth "local endpoint" (no key), default_model none
  → must pass `--model gemma4:12b`. Login: `jcode login --provider ollama` then
  `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '<prompt>'`.
- Differentiators vs Hermes: single-server multi-client daemon (Unix socket, hot-reload), semantic
  memory (local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings + petgraph), swarm (multi-agent same repo), MCP + hooks.
- Maturity caveats: ~277k LOC, **1258 prod `unwrap`/`expect`** (panic risk), 50 files >1200 LOC,
  version mismatch (Cargo 0.31.2 vs badge v0.9.1888-dev), no real stable release, bus factor 1.
  → fine as personal experiment, risky for "prod" reliance.

## Experiment state (2026-06-25)
- Clone: scratchpad temp only (`.../scratchpad/jcode`), NOT in `$WS`. 503 MB.
- Installed **Rust 1.96.0 via Homebrew** (`brew install rust`, pulled `pkgconf` dep) — was absent.
  Removable via `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` if abandoning.
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` → OK 4m56s, 94 MB binary (`target/release/jcode`).
- Telemetry: jcode collects anonymous stats by default → opt out with `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (also `JCODE_NO_TELEMETRY`).
- **Smoke-test PASSED** (2026-06-25): `DO_NOT_TRACK=1 jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '...'`
  works end-to-end; `ollama ps` confirms gemma4 loaded 100% GPU, jcode sets num_ctx 16384. No `jcode login`
  needed for ollama (local endpoint, no key).
- **Latency finding**: warm one-liner = **~11s**, cold = ~65s. BUT jcode runs gemma4 with **thinking ON by
  default** (emits `💭` reasoning even for trivial prompts) → that's the slowness, NOT harness weight.
  jcode HAS the lever (`[provider] openai_reasoning_effort` + per-provider `supports_reasoning_effort`,
  provider-core `set_reasoning_effort`) but does NOT send reasoning_effort=none to ollama by default →
  same trap Hermes already fixed (Ollama /v1 honors `reasoning_effort`, ignores `think`). NOT yet tuned in jcode.
- **THINKING-OFF TUNING (works, 2026-06-25)**: `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` env
  injects the field into the ollama /v1 body → gemma4 thinking OFF (zero `💭`, code only). Same lever Hermes
  uses. To persist for real use: bake into config.toml `[providers.<name>] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`
  on a named ollama provider, or export the env in the launcher. Result warm: thinking-ON 11s → **thinking-OFF 8s**
  (stable ×2); cold ~60s. The 8s is one-shot `jcode run` (re-pays server connect + prompt-eval each call);
  interactive TUI on the persistent warm server would feel snappier.
- **KEY VERDICT**: same model + same Ollama = same decode floor (~7.6 tok/s) regardless of harness. Tuned
  thinking-off, jcode ≈ Hermes on gemma4 speed (both model-bound) — jcode TIES, never beats. So the switch
  decision is NOT about speed; it's about jcode's other edges (RAM ~27 MB vs Hermes Python/Node, boot/UI,
  semantic memory, swarm) vs Hermes' maturity + already-tuned setup. If faster gemma4 is the real goal, the
  lever is a smaller model, not the harness.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.

## Image input (vision) — gemma4 OK, jcode macOS attach BROKEN (2026-06-25)
- gemma4:12b Ollama capabilities = completion, **vision**, audio, tools, thinking (clip projector). Vision REAL:
  direct `POST /v1/chat/completions` with `image_url` data-uri described the screenshot correctly in **4s**.
  So model + OpenAI image protocol both fine.
- jcode TUI image input = clipboard paste only (no `--image` flag on `run`). macOS read path
  (`jcode-tui/.../helpers.rs::clipboard_image`, `#[cfg(macos)]`) shells an embedded **AppleScript**
  (NSPasteboard→PNG). On this **Italian-locale** Mac that AppleScript **errors** ("nome classe plurale",
  -2741) → returns nothing → falls back to arboard (unverified). Net: pasting a fresh screenshot does NOT
  attach → user sees "doesn't accept images". Also terminals can hijack Cmd/Super+V (paste = the keybind).
- Verdict: image input is a real macOS friction/bug for our setup, NOT a gemma4 limit. Fixable (patch the
  applescript / rely on arboard / add a `run --image` flag) but = single-author immaturity tax. Workaround
  today: an image URL pastes/downloads; local screenshots have no good jcode path yet.
- **FIX APPLIED (2026-06-25)**: rewrote the macOS `clipboard_image()` branch in
  `crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/helpers.rs` from AppleScript-ObjC → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`,
  NSPasteboard→NSImage→PNG type 4). JXA syntax is locale-independent → no more -2741. Verified standalone:
  extracts a clipboard screenshot to valid PNG. Rebuilt (incremental 7.9s), JXA marker baked in binary.
  **Trigger is Ctrl+V, NOT Cmd+V**: `is_clipboard_paste_shortcut` = `v/V` + CONTROL|ALT|SUPER|META, but
  macOS terminals hijack Cmd+V (paste text, image dropped via bracketed paste → `handle_paste` skips
  clipboard_image by design). Ctrl+V reaches jcode → smart paste → JXA → image attaches. Patch is a local
  scratchpad edit (not committed upstream); lost if scratchpad cleaned or repo re-cloned.
- **FIX #2 — Cmd+V (the one the user actually wants), 2026-06-25**: user reported Cmd+V attaches images in
  EVERY other agent in the same VS Code terminal, only jcode failed → so the terminal does NOT eat Cmd+V.
  jcode logs (`~/.jcode/logs/jcode-*.log`) proved it: Cmd+V with an image emits `Text paste: 0 chars, 0 lines`
  — VS Code delivers an EMPTY bracketed paste, and jcode's `handle_paste` (input.rs) deliberately skipped
  `clipboard_image()` on bracketed pastes (old Wayland multi-MIME false-positive guard) → image dropped.
  Other agents read the clipboard image on an empty paste; jcode didn't. **Fix**: in `handle_paste`, when
  `text.is_empty()` → `spawn_clipboard_paste(ImageOnly)` (reads clipboard image via the JXA path, attaches).
  Safe because it only fires on EMPTY text (nothing to misidentify); non-empty pastes keep the old guard.
  → Cmd+V now works like every other agent; Ctrl+V still works too. NO keybindings.json / terminal remap
  needed (that idea was wrong — left untouched). Both patches are local scratchpad edits; good upstream-PR
  candidates (helpers.rs JXA + input.rs empty-paste image).

## mermaid-rs-renderer (separate repo, asked 2026-06-25)
https://github.com/1jehuang/mermaid-rs-renderer — same author, standalone Rust mermaid renderer (no browser/TS,
~1800× faster). Already an OPTIONAL git dep of jcode (`jcode-tui-mermaid` feature `renderer`, tag v0.2.1, default
OFF → our build excluded it). Not needed separately for the jcode eval; only worth adopting standalone if we want
fast mermaid rendering in OTHER tools (Hermes/pocket-llm/a CLI). Noted, not actioned.

## Rollback baseline — Hermes (DO NOT switch until eval passes + user OK)
Current Hermes = the thing jcode would replace. See [[hermes-agent-docs]] for full config. Key points:
- Home `~/.hermes`: `config.yaml`, `SOUL.md`, profiles, source checkout `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`.
- Two profiles: `default` → gemma4:12b local (Chip / pocket-llm ext); `openrouter` → agy/Gemini (FreeCode).
- Launchers: `freecode` in `ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`; VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  vscode-launcher) runs `freecode`. The real cutover = repoint these launchers/button from Hermes to jcode.
- **SCOPE CONSTRAINT (user, 2026-06-25): cutover ONLY the local gemma4 lane** (Hermes `default` profile /
  Chip / pocket-llm). The **Free Router lane MUST NOT be touched** (`openrouter` profile / agy/Gemini /
  freecode / robot button stay on Hermes). jcode replaces ONLY the gemma4-local agent.
- Source patch (thinking/temp) in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` via pocket-llm reapply script.
- **Before any cutover**: back up `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (+ profiles) and snapshot launcher files so we
  can revert to Hermes if jcode underperforms.

[[hermes-agent-docs]] [[pocket-llm]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode eval, replace hermes, lighter harness, gemma4 jcode, ollama jcode, rust coding agent, 1jehuang, cutover hermes, rollback hermes
**Why:** user wants to A/B jcode vs Hermes for the local coding lane; needs a clean revert path if jcode loses.
**How to apply:** continue the experiment from "Experiment state"; never cutover launchers without backing up `~/.hermes` first + explicit user approval. If abandoning jcode, `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + delete scratchpad clone.

lines: name: jcode-eval
-description: Evaluating jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) as a lighter replacement for Hermes driving local gemma4. In-flight experiment + Hermes rollback baseline.
+description: jcode eval → ADOPTED for the local gemma4 lane (2026-06-25). Decision rationale + measured findings. Operational details in [[jcode-fork]].
metadata:
type: project
-# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement
+# OUTCOME (2026-06-25): ADOPTED for the local gemma4 lane
+After the full eval below, decision flipped from "keep Hermes" to **adopt jcode** for local gemma4. The
+deciding factor: jcode has **native multi-agent/swarm** (verified implemented — `jcode-swarm-core` channels/
+DM/broadcast + `jcode-terminal-launch` + TUI widgets; the "Proposed" doc header is stale vs the code), which the
+user's trimmed Hermes lacks (delegation disabled for speed). Speed parity (model-bound) was NOT the tiebreaker;
+the new capability + user's willingness to own a fork (neutralizes the bus-factor/maintenance objection) was.
+- Two earlier "keep Hermes" Haiku agents were right on maturity but under-weighted swarm (one misread the stale doc).
+- **Split**: local gemma4 = SOLO jcode; **Hermes stays ONLY for the ccr-free-router Gemini/FreeCode lane**.
+- **Done**: fork `RaggioS/jcode` (branch `feat/local-gemma4-macos`, PR #1) with the 2 macOS image patches +
+  PATCHES.md; pocket-llm switched to jcode launcher (`RaggioS/pocket-llm` PR #16). Operational ref → [[jcode-fork]].
+- Caveat carried forward: gemma4 swarm on one Mac serializes (GPU-bound) → point swarm workers at OpenRouter free.
+# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement (history)
Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model
coding harness, driving `gemma4:12b` on Ollama (or a future local model). Hypothesis: jcode is
much lighter/faster (Rust, ~27 MB RAM embedding-off vs Hermes' Python/Node stack), so response

# Swarm Memory Index — read first every session, before codebase
Retrieval: CLAUDE.md → MEMORY.md → `<type>_<topic>.md` on demand.

## 👤 Profile
- [user_raimondo_spatola.md](user_raimondo_spatola.md) — profile, language/style prefs, "brain first", session-memory preference.

## 📂 Active projects
- [reference_active_projects.md](reference_active_projects.md) — local projects, macOS env, Node/Python, default port, memory paths, config-sync remote + auto-sync.
- [reference_gemini_cli.md](reference_gemini_cli.md) — Gemini REST (`gemini-3.5-flash`): bash invocation, parallel fan-out, when to dispatch as cross-model worker. Key format now `AQ.` (not `AIza` — obsolete).

- [reference_caveman.md](reference_caveman.md) — Caveman token-compression: modes/commands, macOS install/uninstall, config paths. **Installed, full always on.**
- [reference_ccr_openrouter.md](reference_ccr_openrouter.md) — CCR free-only: **Gemini 3.5 Flash PRIMARY** (own key) + cross-provider failover to OpenRouter free via custom router (`ccr-router.js`) + `gemini-failover.js` (429=quota long / 503=overload short cooldown). OpenRouter side = self-healing `fallback-models.js` with **power matrix/classifica** (best-1M + best-lower, score→params ranking). Privacy data-policy gotcha. Tracked in `.claude/ccr/`.
- [reference_ccr_free_router_repo.md](reference_ccr_free_router_repo.md) — private GitHub repo `RaggioS/ccr-free-router` = ACTIVE dev repo for the CCR free-LLM setup. Chain: **Antigravity CLI `agy` (Gemini 3.5 Flash, personal OAuth) → OpenRouter** via `agy-shim.js` local provider, + UI-selection override hook (commit `18c71be`). gemini-cli STILL dead (IneligibleTier→Antigravity) but its replacement `agy` works → wrapped; old Gemini REST path dropped. Verified live e2e 2026-06-24.
- [reference_claude_md.md](reference_claude_md.md) — CLAUDE.md project setup and task guidance.

## 💻 Engineering & Frontend
- [feedback_frontend_preferences.md](feedback_frontend_preferences.md) — dark HSL, glassmorphism, Lucide, Vanilla CSS, React patterns, lint/build gate.
- [feedback_git_workflow.md](feedback_git_workflow.md) — conventional commits EN, branch naming, rebase from main, files not to commit (`session_state.json`); **worktree-per-session/task** (1 session=1 worktree=1 branch=1 PR; same-dir = shared index/branch corruption; `/wt-task` skill A/B; standing rule: `.worktrees/` in every project repo's `.gitignore` (safe PR flow). Validated e2e 2026-06-19 → MEMORY_ARCHIVE.
- [feedback_bilingual_localization_swarm_memory.md](feedback_bilingual_localization_swarm_memory.md) — EN/IT i18n, `titleEn`/`titleIt` props, 7-section recap, auto-archive at Step 10.
> **Engineering standards**: document every component (never empty desc); never hardcode IDs (metadata/labels/dynamic lookups); no queries/writes in loops; handlers delegate to handler classes; check existing component before creating; every PR a product-oriented summary (value/behaviour/impact, not just mechanics; 2-4 sentences) + assignee `RaggioS` — never empty body/bare title; confirm scope (1-2 sentences) before writing code.

## 🤖 AI protocol
- [feedback_pipeline_review.md](feedback_pipeline_review.md) — 2-phase Pipeline Review: A ideation, B post-code; self-review scope; severity discipline.
- [feedback_subagent_orchestration.md](feedback_subagent_orchestration.md) — tier delegation, inline vs sub-agent thresholds, brief (announce type+model+task before dispatch).
- [feedback_deployment_lifecycle.md](feedback_deployment_lifecycle.md) — env dev→staging→prod, no-auto-deploy, pre-deploy checklist, no-polling.
- [feedback_plan_mode_discipline.md](feedback_plan_mode_discipline.md) — never auto-switch plan; declare assumptions before acting; 3-layer task tracking.
- [feedback_bash_cwd_hooks.md](feedback_bash_cwd_hooks.md) — never `cd` in compound Bash (moves persistent CWD → breaks relative-path hooks → deadlock); use subshell; keep hook paths absolute via `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`.
- [feedback_file_deletion_safety.md](feedback_file_deletion_safety.md) — never delete Claude/ccr-adjacent files (hooks, `.claude/**`, tooling symlinks like caveman, ccr config); only explicit unambiguous junk, ask first otherwise.

- [project_subscription_sharing.md](project_subscription_sharing.md) — learning MVP distributed inference mesh (FastAPI coordinator + WS workers, toggle, mock backend); private repo, own remote; roadmap.
- [reference_distributed_inference.md](reference_distributed_inference.md) — RAM-pooling tools (exo / DwarfStar-ds4 / Petals) + quantization reality (DeepSeek-V4 284B can't fit 8GB at any quant; bandwidth = bottleneck, tensor-parallel non-viable on consumer net).
- [project_pocket_llm.md](project_pocket_llm.md) — local Hermes coding agent on Ollama **gemma4:12b** (VS Code launcher); qwen3-vl:8b evaluated & rejected 2026-06-23 (gemma4 wins benchmarks + thinking variant too slow on 16GB); private repo, own remote; symlink shared-config pattern.
- [reference_hermes_agent.md](reference_hermes_agent.md) — Hermes Agent docs/repo + local source `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (don't patch, update overwrites); `--tui` vs `--cli`, key slash/CLI commands, what we disabled for prefill; **local model = `custom` provider (not `ollama`), switch via `hermes model` (cache-validated); gemma4 thinking toggle SOLVED via custom-provider plugin patch (Ollama≥0.30 /v1 honors `reasoning_effort`; `/reasoning none|low|medium|high` works; re-apply patch after `hermes update`)**. **PRIMARY model now = agy (Antigravity Gemini) via the agy-shim, NOT gemma4 (2026-06-24); launcher `freecode` (~/.zshrc) = HERMES sole daily driver, no Claude Code; gemma4 = switchable offline fallback. Text-only caveat (no tool_use over agy).**
- [reference_odysseus_eval.md](reference_odysseus_eval.md) — Odysseus (PewDiePie self-hosted web AI workspace, NOT an LLM) evaluated as full Hermes replacement → **NOT adopted** (2026-06-25): both lanes aggancieable (Ollama gemma4 + OpenRouter/agy custom endpoint), but paradigm mismatch (browser workspace vs editor-embedded TUI) → coexistence > replacement. Pointer kept in case we revisit.
- [project_jcode_eval.md](project_jcode_eval.md) — **IN-FLIGHT** (2026-06-25): jcode (Rust coding-agent harness, MIT, 1jehuang) evaluated as **lighter Hermes replacement** driving local gemma4:12b. Built in scratchpad (Rust 1.96 via brew), BYO-model ollama profile. Hermes UNTOUCHED = rollback baseline (`~/.hermes` + `freecode`/robot-button launchers); no cutover until eval passes + user OK + `~/.hermes` backup.


## 🧩 Skills / Commands / Agents (synced in repo)
- Skills (`.claude/skills/`): save-memory, session-recap, self-review, ticket-close.
- Commands (`.claude/commands/`): /five, /markitdown, /mermaid, /todo, /ux.
- Agents (`.claude/agents/`): coder-reviewer, frontend-engineer, project-manager, tech-lead-architect, ux-designer.
- Caveman (global plugin): /caveman, /caveman-compress, /caveman-stats — full always on.

## 🗂️ Archive
*(empty — closed tickets moved to MEMORY_ARCHIVE.md)*

=== install_release.sh head ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Install the current release binary into the immutable version store,
# update the stable + current channel symlinks, and point the launcher at current.
#
# Paths after install:
# - ~/.jcode/builds/versions/<hash>/jcode (immutable)
# - ~/.jcode/builds/stable/jcode -> .../versions/<hash>/jcode
# - ~/.jcode/builds/current/jcode -> .../versions/<hash>/jcode
# - ~/.local/bin/jcode -> ~/.jcode/builds/current/jcode (launcher)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)"

profile="${JCODE_RELEASE_PROFILE:-release-lto}"
if [[ "${1:-}" == "--fast" ]]; then
  profile="release"
  shift
fi

if [[ "$#" -gt 0 ]]; then
  echo "Usage: $0 [--fast]" >&2
  exit 1
fi

case "$profile" in

content.text: Importante: jcode **auto-genera** un `~/.jcode/config.toml` ricco (keybindings, swarm=true, memory=true). Il mio template full-file lo **sovrascriverebbe** → footgun. Lo correggo in **snippet da MERGEare** (come faceva `hermes/config.snippet.yaml`). Riscrivo:

input.content:
# Pocket LLM — jcode config snippet for a LOCAL Ollama model (Gemma).
#
# jcode auto-generates a full ~/.jcode/config.toml (keybindings, [features] swarm/memory, etc.) on first
# run — do NOT overwrite it. MERGE the two pieces below instead:
#   1. set `default_provider` under the existing [provider] table;
#   2. append the [providers.ollama-local] block (+ its [[...models]]).
# This makes a bare `jcode` default to local gemma4 with thinking OFF and no API key. (The VS Code launcher
# pins `--provider ollama --model gemma4:12b` + JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY, so it works even without this.)

# --- 1. add this line to the existing [provider] table ---
# [provider]
# default_provider = "ollama-local"

# --- 2. append this named provider ---
[providers.ollama-local]
base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"
default_model = "gemma4:12b"
# Ollama ignores the API key but the OpenAI client wants one; a literal placeholder is fine.
api_key = "ollama"
# gemma4 is hybrid-thinking. Ollama's /v1 ignores the native `think` flag but honors a top-level
# `reasoning_effort`. "none" turns reasoning OFF (fast); flip to low|medium|high to turn it on.
extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }

# Pin the context window so jcode does not fall back to the generic 200k default
# (the launcher starts Ollama with a RAM-safe num_ctx of 16384 on a 16GB Mac).
[[providers.ollama-local.models]]
id = "gemma4:12b"
context_window = 16384

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/raimondospatola/Documents/AI Projects/pocket-llm/jcode/config.snippet.toml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

# pocket-llm

Run a capable local AI coding agent directly in VS Code — using **[jcode](https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode)**
(a fast Rust harness, BYO-model) driven by **Ollama** with **Gemma 4 12B** (or any local model).
Fully offline, zero API tokens.

This repository contains:
1. **`vscode-extension/`** — a one-click **launcher** (status-bar button). It manages the Ollama
   server lifecycle (auto-start, keep-warm, RAM-aware context length), pre-warms the model, then
   opens the native **jcode TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`jcode/`** — our jcode configuration for local Gemma: `config.toml` (local Ollama provider,
   thinking-off, telemetry-off) and `AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona). Drop these into `~/.jcode/`
   and `~/AGENTS.md`. See `vscode-extension/JCODE.md` for the details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **jcode does the agent work**: tools, file edits, multi-session server, swarm/multi-agent,
  semantic memory — a lightweight Rust harness (~27MB RAM, instant boot) so the overhead never
  competes with the model for unified memory.
* **Native jcode TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/warms everything and drops you straight
  into jcode in a parallel editor column — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Thinking off on a small model**: gemma4 is hybrid-thinking; on a 16GB Mac long reasoning kills
  responsiveness, so we default `reasoning_effort = none` (warm turns ~8s). Flip it back on per turn.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **jcode** — build + install our fork (carries a macOS image-paste fix):
  ```bash
  git clone https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode.git
  cd jcode
  cargo build --release --bin jcode
  scripts/install_release.sh   # → ~/.jcode/builds/current + ~/.local/bin/jcode
  ```
  Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in `PATH`.

### 2. Apply our jcode config
- Copy `jcode/config.toml` → `~/.jcode/config.toml` (local Ollama provider, `gemma4:12b`,
  thinking off via `extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"`).
- Copy `jcode/AGENTS.md` → `~/AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona, loaded into every system prompt).

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run "Scrivi una funzione python che somma due numeri. Solo codice."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~8s on a 16GB Mac, thinking off.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

---

## How to use

1. Open a workspace folder in VS Code.
2. Click **$(rocket) jcode** in the status bar (or run the `Avvia jcode (Pocket LLM)` command).
3. The launcher auto-starts Ollama (warm, RAM-sized context), pre-warms the model, and opens a
   terminal as a **parallel editor column** (beside your code) running the jcode TUI.
4. Type your task and press **Enter**. jcode thinks, edits files in the workspace, and runs
   commands via its own tools. Paste a screenshot with **Cmd+V**.
5. Done for now? Run `Pocket LLM: ferma server Ollama` to free the RAM.

> Note: we do **not** ship models. Pull them with `ollama pull`. jcode lives in `~/.jcode`
> (binary in `~/.local/bin/jcode`); this repo carries only our config + the VS Code panel.

---

## Tuning knobs
- `~/.jcode/config.toml` — `default_model` (any local Ollama tag), `extra_body.reasoning_effort`
  (`none`/`low`/`medium`/`high`), `context_window`. Thinking off = snappiest on a local 12B.
- `~/AGENTS.md` — the always-on persona/instructions. Per-project `AGENTS.md` adds project rules.
- Context length / keep-alive are set automatically by the extension when it launches Ollama.

opens the native **jcode TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
code, not in the bottom panel.

+   `AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona → `~/AGENTS.md`). See `vscode-extension/JCODE.md` for the details.

   opens the native **jcode TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`jcode/`** — our jcode configuration for local Gemma: `config.snippet.toml` (local Ollama provider,
   thinking-off — **merge** into the auto-generated `~/.jcode/config.toml`, don't overwrite it) and
   `AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona → `~/AGENTS.md`). See `vscode-extension/JCODE.md` for the details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **jcode does the agent work**: tools, file edits, multi-session server, swarm/multi-agent,
  semantic memory — a lightweight Rust harness (~27MB RAM, instant boot) so the overhead never
  competes with the model for unified memory.
* **Native jcode TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/warms everything and drops you straight
  into jcode in a parallel editor column — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Thinking off on a small model**: gemma4 is hybrid-thinking; on a 16GB Mac long reasoning kills
  responsiveness, so we default `reasoning_effort = none` (warm turns ~8s). Flip it back on per turn.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **jcode** — build + install our fork (carries a macOS image-paste fix):
  ```bash
  git clone https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode.git
  cd jcode
  cargo build --release --bin jcode
  scripts/install_release.sh   # → ~/.jcode/builds/current + ~/.local/bin/jcode
  ```
  Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in `PATH`.

### 2. Apply our jcode config
- Copy `jcode/config.toml` → `~/.jcode/config.toml` (local Ollama provider, `gemma4:12b`,
  thinking off via `extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"`).
- Copy `jcode/AGENTS.md` → `~/AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona, loaded into every system prompt).

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run "Scrivi una funzione python che somma due numeri. Solo codice."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~8s on a 16GB Mac, thinking off.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

---

## How to use

1. Open a workspace folder in VS Code.
2. Click **$(rocket) jcode** in the status bar (or run the `Avvia jcode (Pocket LLM)` command).
3. The launcher auto-starts Ollama (warm, RAM-sized context), pre-warms the model, and opens a
   terminal as a **parallel editor column** (beside your code) running the jcode TUI.
4. Type your task and press **Enter**. jcode thinks, edits files in the workspace, and runs
   commands via its own tools. Paste a screenshot with **Cmd+V**.
5. Done for now? Run `Pocket LLM: ferma server Ollama` to free the RAM.

> Note: we do **not** ship models. Pull them with `ollama pull`. jcode lives in `~/.jcode`
> (binary in `~/.local/bin/jcode`); this repo carries only our config + the VS Code panel.

---

## Tuning knobs
- `~/.jcode/config.toml` — `default_model` (any local Ollama tag), `extra_body.reasoning_effort`
  (`none`/`low`/`medium`/`high`), `context_window`. Thinking off = snappiest on a local 12B.
- `~/AGENTS.md` — the always-on persona/instructions. Per-project `AGENTS.md` adds project rules.
- Context length / keep-alive are set automatically by the extension when it launches Ollama.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# jcode setup notes (Pocket LLM)

The launcher opens the native **jcode** TUI (Rust harness, BYO-model) against local
Ollama `gemma4:12b`. jcode is installed separately; this repo carries only our config.

## Install jcode (our fork)

We run a personal fork that carries a macOS image-paste fix (see the fork's `PATCHES.md`):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode.git
cd jcode
cargo build --release --bin jcode
scripts/install_release.sh        # → ~/.jcode/builds/current + ~/.local/bin/jcode
```

Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in `PATH`.

## Our config

- **`jcode/config.toml`** → copy to `~/.jcode/config.toml`. Sets the local Ollama provider,
  `gemma4:12b` default, and `extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"` (thinking OFF, fast).
- **`jcode/AGENTS.md`** → copy to `~/AGENTS.md`. Lean Italian persona, loaded into every
  system prompt (jcode reads `~/AGENTS.md` + project `AGENTS.md`).
- **`jcode/env.sample`** → only if you run `jcode` by hand without the config defaults.

## Thinking toggle

gemma4 is hybrid-thinking. Ollama's `/v1` ignores the native `think` flag but honors a
top-level `reasoning_effort`. Our config sets it to `none` (OFF). Flip it live in the TUI
with jcode's reasoning controls, or change `extra_body.reasoning_effort` in `config.toml`.

## Telemetry

jcode collects anonymous usage stats by default. `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (set by the launcher and
in `env.sample`) disables it.

## Images

Screenshot in the clipboard → **Cmd+V** (or Ctrl+V) in the jcode TUI attaches it. This relies
on the fork's two macOS clipboard patches; stock upstream jcode drops images on macOS-Italian.

-- **`jcode/config.toml`** → copy to `~/.jcode/config.toml`. Sets the local Ollama provider,
-  `gemma4:12b` default, and `extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"` (thinking OFF, fast).
+- **`jcode/config.snippet.toml`** → **merge** into `~/.jcode/config.toml` (jcode auto-generates that file;
+  don't overwrite). Sets the local Ollama provider, `gemma4:12b` default, and
+  `extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"` (thinking OFF, fast).
- **`jcode/AGENTS.md`** → copy to `~/AGENTS.md`. Lean Italian persona, loaded into every
system prompt (jcode reads `~/AGENTS.md` + project `AGENTS.md`).
- **`jcode/env.sample`** → only if you run `jcode` by hand without the config defaults.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# pocket-llm

Run a capable local AI coding agent directly in VS Code — using **[jcode](https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode)**
(a fast Rust harness, BYO-model) driven by **Ollama** with **Gemma 4 12B** (or any local model).
Fully offline, zero API tokens.

This repository contains:
1. **`vscode-extension/`** — a one-click **launcher** (status-bar button). It manages the Ollama
   server lifecycle (auto-start, keep-warm, RAM-aware context length), pre-warms the model, then
   opens the native **jcode TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`jcode/`** — our jcode configuration for local Gemma: `config.snippet.toml` (local Ollama provider,
   thinking-off — **merge** into the auto-generated `~/.jcode/config.toml`, don't overwrite it) and
   `AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona → `~/AGENTS.md`). See `vscode-extension/JCODE.md` for the details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **jcode does the agent work**: tools, file edits, multi-session server, swarm/multi-agent,
  semantic memory — a lightweight Rust harness (~27MB RAM, instant boot) so the overhead never
  competes with the model for unified memory.
* **Native jcode TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/warms everything and drops you straight
  into jcode in a parallel editor column — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Thinking off on a small model**: gemma4 is hybrid-thinking; on a 16GB Mac long reasoning kills
  responsiveness, so we default `reasoning_effort = none` (warm turns ~8s). Flip it back on per turn.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **jcode** — build + install our fork (carries a macOS image-paste fix):
  ```bash
  git clone https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode.git
  cd jcode
  cargo build --release --bin jcode
  scripts/install_release.sh   # → ~/.jcode/builds/current + ~/.local/bin/jcode
  ```
  Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in `PATH`.

### 2. Apply our jcode config
- **Merge** `jcode/config.snippet.toml` into `~/.jcode/config.toml` (jcode auto-generates that file on first
  run — set `default_provider = "ollama-local"` under `[provider]` and append the `[providers.ollama-local]`
  block; do NOT overwrite the file). Gives `gemma4:12b` + thinking off (`extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"`).
- Copy `jcode/AGENTS.md` → `~/AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona, loaded into every system prompt).

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run "Scrivi una funzione python che somma due numeri. Solo codice."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~8s on a 16GB Mac, thinking off.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

# pocket-llm

Run a capable local AI coding agent directly in VS Code — using **[jcode](https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode)**
(a fast Rust harness, BYO-model) driven by **Ollama** with **Gemma 4 12B** (or any local model).
Fully offline, zero API tokens.

This repository contains:
1. **`vscode-extension/`** — a one-click **launcher** (status-bar button). It manages the Ollama
   server lifecycle (auto-start, keep-warm, RAM-aware context length), pre-warms the model, then
   opens the native **jcode TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`jcode/`** — our jcode configuration for local Gemma: `config.snippet.toml` (local Ollama provider,
   thinking-off — **merge** into the auto-generated `~/.jcode/config.toml`, don't overwrite it) and
   `AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona → `~/AGENTS.md`). See `vscode-extension/JCODE.md` for the details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **jcode does the agent work**: tools, file edits, multi-session server, swarm/multi-agent,
  semantic memory — a lightweight Rust harness (~27MB RAM, instant boot) so the overhead never
  competes with the model for unified memory.
* **Native jcode TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/warms everything and drops you straight
  into jcode in a parallel editor column — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Thinking off on a small model**: gemma4 is hybrid-thinking; on a 16GB Mac long reasoning kills
  responsiveness, so we default `reasoning_effort = none` (warm turns ~8s). Flip it back on per turn.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **jcode** — build + install our fork (carries a macOS image-paste fix):
  ```bash
  git clone https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode.git
  cd jcode
  cargo build --release --bin jcode
  scripts/install_release.sh   # → ~/.jcode/builds/current + ~/.local/bin/jcode
  ```
  Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in `PATH`.

### 2. Apply our jcode config
- **Merge** `jcode/config.snippet.toml` into `~/.jcode/config.toml` (jcode auto-generates that file on first
  run — set `default_provider = "ollama-local"` under `[provider]` and append the `[providers.ollama-local]`
  block; do NOT overwrite the file). Gives `gemma4:12b` + thinking off (`extra_body.reasoning_effort = "none"`).
- Copy `jcode/AGENTS.md` → `~/AGENTS.md` (lean Italian persona, loaded into every system prompt).

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run "Write a Python function that adds two numbers. Code only."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~8s on a 16GB Mac, thinking off.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

---

## How to use

1. Open a workspace folder in VS Code.
2. Click **$(rocket) jcode** in the status bar (or run the `Avvia jcode (Pocket LLM)` command).
3. The launcher auto-starts Ollama (warm, RAM-sized context), pre-warms the model, and opens a
   terminal as a **parallel editor column** (beside your code) running the jcode TUI.
4. Type your task and press **Enter**. jcode thinks, edits files in the workspace, and runs
   commands via its own tools. Paste a screenshot with **Cmd+V**.
5. Done for now? Run `Pocket LLM: ferma server Ollama` to free the RAM.

> Note: we do **not** ship models. Pull them with `ollama pull`. jcode lives in `~/.jcode`
> (binary in `~/.local/bin/jcode`); this repo carries only our config + the VS Code panel.

---

- `~/.jcode/config.toml` — `default_model` (any local Ollama tag), `extra_body.reasoning_effort`

- `~/AGENTS.md` — the always-on persona/instructions. Per-project `AGENTS.md` adds project rules.
- Context length / keep-alive are set automatically by the extension when it launches Ollama.
